name: ZD 2017 Founder's Reserve Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: A fruity, inviting nose of dark cherry and baking spice lead the way to a complex, textured and richly layered midpalate of integrated intensity and dried herb in this reserve-worthy wine. Robust and hearty on the palate it retains a welcome freshness and lasting note of sweet oak.

name: Zaca Mesa 2017 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Broad black-cherry and macerated strawberry aromas meet with plenty of clove and cardamom on the soft, lush nose of this bottling. The palate offers a broad range of flavors from Montmorency cherry to white pepper, eucalyptus and bay leaf.

name: YNOT 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Mulberry, macerated wild cherry, cherry pit and dusty garam masala spices show on the nose of this bottling, Norm Yost's affordable appellation blend. There's a black-plum tang to the sip, where cardamom and curry spices decorate the roasted tomato and black-cherry flavors.

name: Writer's Block 2017 Pinot Noir (Lake County)
review: A good choice in a pleasant, well-mannered wine, this bottle offers just-ripe black-cherry and cinnamon flavors, medium body and a smooth texture.

name: Work Vineyard 2017 Zina's Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This hearty, thick wine shows the warmth of the vintage, thick in cinnamon cola and waves of full-bodied blueberry lushness.

name: Windrun 2017 No. 15 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Beautifully candied dark-cherry aromas meet with vanilla, nutmeg and cardamom on the lush, inviting nose of this bottling. The sip snaps with strong raspberry and rose-petals flavors that ride a very fresh acidity into a lively finish.

name: Windchaser 2017 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Nicely ripe and well balanced at the same time, this full-bodied wine is scented with cherries and baking spices. Fermented with native yeast, it brings a good grip of tannins and acidity to the palate.

name: Willowbrook 2017 Kaufman Sunnyslope Vineyard Clone 777 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)
review: Flavors of black tea, strawberry and black cherry ride along a smooth, velvety palate. While well-structured, it also retains plenty of lift and delicacy throughout.

name: Wildeye 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Dark red plum, minty eucalyptus, wet gravel and tar aromas show on the nose of this blend of the Pommard and 777 clones. Concentrated cherry and berry flavors show on the sip, enhanced by wild thyme and zesty acidity.

name: Westwood 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: This is a bold, hugely concentrated wine, jammy in up-front notions of black cherry and baked strawberry. With firm silky tannins that add weight and structure, it shows its oak, letting it settle to reveal appealing accents of dried herb and Asian spice.

name: Westerhold 2017 Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This wine is made by Russell Bevan, who sources from the proprietor's hillside vineyard between several mountain peaks, benefiting from coastal air and morning fog. Thick, spicy and herbal, it is possessed of substantial oak and ripe rich flavors of baked cherry, strawberry and forest floor.

name: Walt 2017 Brown Ranch Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Soft, supple and thick in cola and brown sugar, this is a deeply concentrated ripe red wine, with dense layers of black cherry. Baking-spice and dark-chocolate notes dot the finish.

name: Walt 2017 Bob's Ranch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is a highly ripe, generously bodied wine, with robust flavors of candied cherry and cola. Earthy stem and forest play around its thick, viscous core of baking spice, finishing with plenty of tannin and oak.

name: Walt 2017 Savoy Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Bright, spicy new-oak aromas and flavors light up this full-bodied wine, and it delivers good, ripe cherry and black-plum flavors to balance out the oak. With a little time in the bottle the unique character of the Savoy vineyard should emerge. Best after 2022.

name: Walt 2017 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Wonderful and complex fruit and oak flavors mingle on a broad warm texture in this full-bodied, flavor-packed and well-balanced wine. Good depth and an attractive layering of black cherry, raspberry and grilled plum make it special.

name: Waits-Mast 2017 Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: This nicely balanced and well-rounded wine offers generous fruit flavors and subtle oak spices. It is medium to full in body, fairly soft in texture and concentrated enough to drive a lingering, complex finish. This new wine is the least expensive Pinot from Waits Mast, blended from Anderson Valley grapes mixed with grapes from other parts of Mendocino County.

name: Vokel 2017 Perezchica Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Cranberry and orange flavors highlight a soft, slightly sweet and quite ripe wine here, which finishes in dried herb.

name: Vokel 2017 828 Neighbors Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This small-production wine is earthy and stemmy, with a silky smooth palate of rhubarb and orange. The acidity is fresh, forceful and in full-command, helping to lighten the full-bodied load.

name: Vokel 2017 Eastside Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This robustly ripe, concentrated and thick wine takes on a sour quality, its tannin profile puckering and leaning somewhat green.

name: Vie Winery 2017 Pinot Noir (North Coast)
review: Good flavors and a slightly tangy texture will make this wine a great accompaniment to food. It offers ripe black-cherry and black-plum flavors and a touch of good tannin in the texture.

name: Valley Vista 2017 Private Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Light cherry and pomegranate flavors are lifted by hints of clove and allspice on the nose of this bottling. There is a pleasing snap of raspberry on the sip, where gingerbread spices emerge, as does a tightly woven texture.

name: V. Sattui 2017 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This mellow, relaxed and full-bodied wine smells delicious, feels supple and polished on the palate and delivers wonderful, ripe black-cherry, sweet oak and black-tea flavors that are practically irresistible. Rather soft in structure, it is something to enjoy soon.

name: Tuli 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: This wine is spicy, earthy and thin in fruit, with faint notions of strawberry and rhubarb popping through on the midpalate. A leathery texture loses steam and structure on the finish.

name: Truchard 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Offering great value and quality, this elegant red is tingly in acidity, with well-integrated tannin and oak and lengthy structure. Black cherry, orange, clove and nutmeg are highlighted on the supple, silky palate, finishing in a swirl of vanilla.

name: Trombetta 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Earthy funk and back tea lead to a velvety smooth core of cherry cola in this brooding, medium-bodied wine, Crisp acidity boosts its inherent richness and denser tendencies.

name: Troika 2017 Berlin Vineyards Pinot Noir (Bennett Valley)
review: Sharp red fruit and citrus make for a fresh earthy experience in this light-bodied wine, thick in tannin and just a hint of toasted oak. Dried herb and black tea come to the fore on the soft supple finish.

name: Toretti 2017 Valentino Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Mellow aromas of nutmeg, sour black cherry and black cardamom kick off the nose of this bottling from a longtime Santa Maria-residing family. Very firm tannins frame the sip, where rich black-cherry flavors meet with nutmeg and vanilla. Drink 2021–2037.

name: Topophilia 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This wine has a hearty earthiness that detracts from the strawberry and cherry fruit—the style full-bodied and robustly ripe with funky oak. Minty eucalyptus is threaded throughout.

name: Tongue Dancer 2017 The Sly One Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This tiny-production wine is made from one barrel from Van Der Kamp Vineyard and another from Placida, aged 29 months in French oak. Thick and extracted blue fruit, clove and earthy allspice combine around an expanse of velvety texture and tannin.

name: Tongue Dancer 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: The grapes for this appellation wine hail from Placida, Van Der Kamp and Putnam—all worthy single-vineyard designates of their own. Together, they form a bold, brawny expression of spicy clove, nutmeg and cola cherry with a twist of orange, the rich texture sleek and soft.

name: Tongue Dancer 2017 Pinot de Ville Putnam Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a favorite site of the producer's high up in Annapolis and just six miles from the ocean, this hearty, robust red is balanced and complex in texture and weight. Dark forest floor, dried herb and a taste of the sea permeate a brooding sense of brambly black fruit, cola and toasted oak.

name: Timbre 2017 Guest Star Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Rich aromas of mesquite-smoked black cherry show on the nose of this bottling from Squire Canyon, a vineyard located just five miles from the Pacific Ocean. The oaky flavors appear on the sip too, where baked cherry, hickory- roasted pork loin and light bacon tones arise.

name: Timbre 2017 Lead Vocals Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: From old vines at Bien Nacido Vineyard, this bottling is light in the glass but loaded with intrigue, offering aromas of muddled strawberry, mulberry and wild plum as well as sumac, carnations and hibiscus on the nose. The palate is zesty and layered with bay leaf and dark spices set against a brisk strawberry and cherry core. It is very easy to sip, yet immensely complex.

name: Timbre 2017 Supergroup Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Roasty hints of mesquite mesh with red cherry, rust and turned soil on the nose of this blend of fruit from Bien Nacido and Presqu'ile vineyards. The structure is firm on the palate, where waves of strawberry and rounded plum meet with clove, sage and light game.

name: Timbre 2017 The Headliner Presqu'ile Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Extreme amounts of cinnamon and chai spice met with a tightly wound raspberry tone on the brilliant nose of this bottling. The palate is very snappy and crisp, with barely ripe raspberry and red-apple flavors, but it's the bay leaf, pine-needle and stinging nettle elements that push this wine to another level.

name: Three Sticks 2017 Gap's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Made from the iconic site, which is also owned by the producer, this wine is richly layered and supremely structured in a wealth of crunchy red fruit and cardamom spice. Tension reigns in the form of sharp, persistent acidity and bold, integrated tannins that add substance and style.

name: Three Sticks 2017 Cuvée Eva Marie Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)
review: This wine is made from select barrels from the producer's own One Sky Vineyard, high above Sonoma Valley at an elevation of 1,400-feet. Intense and tannic, it unwinds slowly, revealing powerful flavors of ripe black cherry, cola and cardamom. Complex and focused, it finishes as intensely as it starts, with a buzz of black tea.

name: Three Sticks 2017 Walala Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a wild, ocean-facing site (an estate property for the producer) this bold, brawny red is as exotic as its provenance, brimming in nervy acidity and robust, structured tannin. Herbal, earthy and savory, its flavor profile is elusive and complex—a mix of salty rock, black tea, anise and black currant.

name: Three Sticks 2017 Durell Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Deliciously balanced and flavorful, this coastal-influenced red from estate-grown grapes delivers rich, elegant red fruit, dried herb and a touch of flint. The oak is well integrated and adds warmth and breadth across the palate, finishing on accents of cardamom and black tea.

name: Three Furies 2017 Constant Vengeance Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Black cherry, wild mint and earthy aromas of crushed rock and turned soil kick off the nose on this appellation blend. Mulberry and wild cherry flavors meet with more crushed rock minerality and minty marjoram tones on the sip.

name: Thomas T Thomas 2017 Reserve Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Quite flavorful and well structured, this full-bodied wine fills the mouth with vivid black-cherry, baking-spice and black-tea notes while moderate tannins and good acidity give it a nice snap on the palate.

name: Thirty-Seven 2017 Reserve Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Toasted oak and earthy stems open into a vast midpalae of fleshy, lush texture and richness, highlighted most prominently in black cherry, strawberry and dried herb. Well made, this wine offers good value, and a reserve-worthy complexity at its core.

name: Thirty-Seven 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A wealth of forest floor, savory oak and buzzy acidity wrap around a midpalate of balanced weight in this intensely earthy, complex wine. A lasting note of toasted oak rides through the finish.

name: The Winery SF 2017 Pinot Noir (North Coast)
review: This is a very enjoyable wine that hits the spot. It offers a lively, structured mouthfeel, classic varietal flavors and good depth. It's appetizing and satisfying and should please a crowd.

name: The West Pole 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A new offering from winemaker Kurt Beitler, this comes from estate-grown grapes, many of them also components of his single-vineyard Bohème wines. Perfumed in plum and cherry, the palate is juicy and textured, with a lovely length and hints of cardamom, clove and oak.

name: The Lost Chapters 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Minty, herbal and stemmy, this big red wine is crisp and fresh up front, opting for a more robustly powerful midpalate of plummy concentration and thick tannin.

name: The Calling 2017 Patriarch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tart red fruit highlights an expression of bright underlying acidity in this full-bodied, richly layered wine, which is expansive on the palate in smooth, rounded edges of flavor and texture. Baking spice, toasted oak and a hint of vanilla underscore the appeal.

name: The Calling 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Intensely earthy, spicy and densely packed in black fruit, this wine is full bodied, fleshy and lengthy, with a generous helping of oak. The powerful nature of it appeals on the palate, with plenty of complexity.

name: The Calling 2017 Sunny View Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a big brawny red wine with plenty of acid beneath its density to remain in balance and bright on the palate. Spicy and layered in black fruit, cola and oak, it delivers a fruit-forward style that will widely appeal.

name: Terragena 2017 Abbassi Vineyard Clone 115 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This single-barrel offering aged in neutral French oak is light and delicate in color and style, with characteristics of mint, orange and rhubarb that highlight its bristling acidity. Earthy and soft, it finishes focused and fresh.

name: Terragena 2017 Abbassi Vineyard Clone Pommard Neutral Oak Pinot Noir (Carneros)
designation: Abbassi Vineyard Clone Pommard Neutral Oak
review: Another of the producer's single-barrel experiments, this is minty and herbal with undefined fruit, as well as soft supple tannin and low oak. It finishes fast and bright.

name: Terragena 2017 Abbassi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Lightly textured and defined in crisp red fruit, this is a high-toned, acid-driven red, with subtle oak and earthiness. A lasting tilt of baking spice seasons flavors of blueberry and black cherry, integrating well in the glass.

name: Temperance 2017 Rio Vista Vineyard Clone 777 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Sandalwood and incense aromas meet with dark plum, cinnamon cookies and dried cherries on the nose of this bottling. Dried cherry, clove and mulling-spice flavors show on the sip.

name: Tehachapi 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This full-bodied wine is packed with oak spices that tend to overwhelm the nearly sweet raspberry and red-cherry flavors. Toasted oak, baking spices and cedar provide most of the substance.

name: Tamber Bey 2017 Walala Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Supple and simple in black cherry and black tea, this wine is robust on the midpalate, with a weighty viscous quality to the finish.

name: Tamber Bey 2017 Sun Chase Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Earthy and leathery, this wine shows plenty of red and blue fruit and bright acidity, fresh elements that contrast well against its essential grip and hearty French oak.

name: Talley 2017 Pinot Noir (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Soft but dense aromas of pomegranate meet with thyme and tarragon on the pleasant nose of this appellation blend. Tart cranberry and sour plum greet an array of herbs on the palate.

name: Talley 2017 Rincon Pinot Noir (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Light and pleasing aromas of pomegranate, candied raspberry, hibiscus and rose petal are bright and beautiful on the nose of this bottling. The palate grows more intense with cranberry and pomegranate flavors set against white pepper and marjoram, with tense tannins and zippy acid that carry into the finish.

name: Talley 2017 Rosemary's Pinot Noir (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Fat black-cherry and plum-skin aromas meet with elegant rose petals on the powerful nose of this bottling. It lands with a tightly wound frame, showing cocoa, sage and black-cherry flavors, finishing on a hint of vanilla.

name: Talbott 2017 RFT Diamond T Vineyard Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Monterey)
designation: RFT Diamond T Vineyard Estate Grown
review: Booming aromas of black cherry, boysenberry, crushed slate, gravel, hibiscus and black tea show on the dynamic nose of this bottling. On the palate, full flavors of cranberry and sour cherry meet with French toast and pastry flake alongside ample accents of oregano and lavender.

name: Talbott 2017 Block 23 West Sleepy Hollow Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Red plum and currant meet with sharp mace and cola on the nose of this block-designated bottling. Thick cherry and raspberry-jam flavors are lifted by rose petals and more baking spice on the boisterous palate.

name: Talbott 2017 Sleepy Hollow Vineyard Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: There are savory iodine and sanguine kicks to the nose of this bottling, with dried boysenberry and leather aromas as well. That earthy, meaty element carries to the sip, where dried cherry and shiitake mushroom flavors show.

name: Talbott 2017 Block 48 South Sleepy Hollow Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Densely packed aromas of black plum, boysenberry, clove and smoked meats carry the nose of this block-designated bottling. A dark cherry-syrup flavor is framed by firm tannins on the sip, where cracked pepper and cardamom cream compete for attention, all framed by a grippy texture and smooth acidity.

name: Talbott 2017 Sarah Case Sleepy Hollow Vineyard Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
designation: Sarah Case Sleepy Hollow Vineyard Estate Grown
review: Dried cherry and berry aromas meet with leather, game and baked earth on the complex nose of this bottling from a famed vineyard—one of the oldest in the appellation. The palate is reminiscent of pork roasted in a cherry compote with cardamom dust, proving delicious and layered.

name: Sundae wines 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Plenty of ripe fruit flavors, and equally plentiful oak spices provide a lot to appreciate in this full-bodied but mellow and supple wine. So don't let the dessert-style brand name be a turn-off.

name: Summerland 2017 Theresa Noelle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: A bit shy on the nose, this single-vineyard expression starts with aromas of cherry and slate. The palate combines cranberry, raspberry and hibiscus with baking spice, finishing with a clean and tart snap.

name: Summerland 2017 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Red cherry and hickory smoke blend the fruit and savory sides of the grape in this appellation blend. Cranberry and more cherry come through on the palate, along with a peppery spice toward the finish, all framed by crisp acidity.

name: Storypoint 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This potent, concentrated and smoky wine shows no resemblance to Pinot Noir. Think of it as a red blend. It is very deep and dark in color, bursts with blueberry and dark-chocolate flavors, is full bodied and dense with tannins.

name: Storm 2017 Presqu'ile Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Dark mulberry and blistered tomato flavors meet with cracked peppercorn, bay leaf and eucalyptus tea on the nose of this bottling from an extremely chilly vineyard. There's a lot of herbal zest to the palate, where tea and tomato-leaf flavors converge with green cardamom, Kalamata olive and brisk, underripe raspberry.

name: Storm 2017 Duvarita Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: There's a dark and ripe sense of boysenberry jam on the nose of this bottling from a unique vineyard that sits west of the Sta. Rita Hills, yet there is also a mineral streak of slate and a zesty citrus element. The palate is invigoratingly grippy, offering cherry, mulberry and star anise flavors that are sliced through with bright acidity.

name: Storm 2017 John Sebastiano Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Wondrously stemmy aromas of cracked peppercorns, seaweed and salted meats combine with black raspberry on the nose of this bottling. The nori wrap flavors continue on the sip, where dank forest-floor moss meets with cranberry and orange-rind elements.

name: Storm 2017 Donnachadh Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: This new estate project is extremely promising, as evidenced in the nose of this bottling, which shows bay leaf, peppercorn, blood, iodine and mulberry on the nose. The palate is savory with peppercorns, crushed marjoram and oregano set against underripe raspberry and lavender.

name: Stewart Cellars 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Racy acidity lies beneath a foundation of baking spice, forest floor, sage and dried herb in this smooth, silky wine. Firm tannins wrap around dark brooding black fruit that is both thick and concentrated but not overtly heavy, finishing in clove.

name: Stephen Ross 2017 Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: This appellation blend offers a more affordable crack at what veteran winemaker Steve Dooley is up to. Focused aromas of boysenberry pie, vanilla, cinnamon and dried tarragon lead into a palate loaded with Bing cherry, cardamom and chai spice.

name: Stephen Ross 2017 Stone Corral Vineyard Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Elegant aromas of packed flowers, smashed raspberry, dried herbs, dark soil and rocky minerality show on the nose of this bottling from a vineyard in the heart of the appellation. The palate hints at game and earth while leading with tangy black-plum and purple-flower flavors.

name: Stephen Ross 2017 Escolle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Elegantly subdued aromas of dried plum, dusty earth and dried violet aromas show on the nose of this bottling from one of the northernmost vineyards in the appellation. The palate is loaded with floral tones, from lavender to lilac to violet, which decorate the underlying purple fruits.

name: Stephen Ross 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Baked cherry, root-beer-candy and caramel aromas show on the nose of this appellation blend. The palate offers more cooked fruit flavors and cola spice, dusted with dried earth tones.

name: Stasis 2017 Murmur Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Baked black-cherry, black-currant and licorice aromas make for a fairly ripe expression from this cool-climate vineyard. It's a boisterous wine on the palate, with ripe boysenberry and mulberry flavors lifted by hints of roasted fennel and cracked pepper.

name: Starmont 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This is well made and fairly priced, offering substantial value for the high quality. Earthy in forest floor, dried herb and black tea, it has a density to the midpalate that resolves and freshens in the glass, with a strong,tannic finish.

name: Sonoma-Loeb 2017 Dutton Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: A brawny, full-bodied wine, this offers an intensity of earthiness and rich black and red berry accented in cinnamon, cola and vanilla. The tannins are integrated and soft, the acidity lingering and high toned enough to stand up to the overall richness.

name: Sonoma-Cutrer 2017 Vine Hill Flanc De Coteau Bloc Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
designation: Vine Hill Flanc De Coteau Bloc
review: Citrus, cranberry and a creamy texture define this limited-production wine, made from the producer's highest-elevation hillside blocks. Balanced, it offers fresh, abundant acidity and supple, integrated tannins, finishing in forest floor, clove and black tea.

name: Sola 2017 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: A browner shade of ruby in the glass, this bottling starts with slightly sweet cherry and dried strawberry aromas along with hints of brown spice and wet earth. Dried cherry and tart plum skins meet with rustic earthy and mushroom flavors on the palate.

name: Sleeping Giant 2017 Dearden Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Rich and velvety in style, this lush red is opulent in blue fruit and baking spice. Well-integrated tannins support the powerful concentration and help to accent the toasted oak and lasting note of vanilla.

name: Sketchbook 2017 Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Great fruit flavors and a good, lip-smacking texture make this wine a winner. Coming from a superreliable producer, it shows vivid red-cherry and black-cherry aromas and mouthfilling cherry and raspberry flavors along with a black tea nuance.

name: Simple Life 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This wine feels comfy and tastes agreeable, matching medium to full body with generous apple-skin, red-cherry and baking-spice flavors on a smooth texture that's light in terms of tannin.

name: Signaterra Benziger 2017 Bella Luna Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Made from Demeter-certified biodynamic grapes, this lovely wine is tangy and thick in tannin, cola spice and black currant. Earthy and dense, it has a strong layers of blueberry and black cherry with tangy acidity bursting on the finish.

name: Siduri 2017 Ewald Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Tasting of strawberry puree, this fanciful, fruit-forward wine is smooth and silky in tannin, with a creamy palate that appeals. Medium bodied, it offers richness within context and balance, finishing in fresh acidity.

name: Siduri 2017 Van Der Kamp Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)
review: Earthy forest and damp earth make it clear this is from a wild, mountainous site, the texture rich and rounded with a pronounced profile of oak and tannin. Red and black cherry combine with the savory characteristics to flesh out the full-bodied concentration.

name: Siduri 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This value-minded wine is a nice showcase of the appellation, offering a mix of forested earth and grilled mushroom. Smooth, supple tannins form around an intense midpalate of red cherry and citrus that remains fresh on a lengthy finish.

name: Siduri 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy black and red fruit highlight a weighty, full-bodied and fruit-forward style of powerful flavor in this well-made appellation wine. Baking spice and cola round out the profile.

name: Siduri 2017 Perry Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Soft and smoothly textured in measured richness, this wine offers rounded flavors of strawberry, dark cherry and cola. With integrated tannin and oak, it presents a seamless experience across the palate, seasoned in hints of black tea and cinnamon.

name: Shooting Star 2017 Pinot Noir (Lake County)
review: Tasting mature and mellow, this medium-bodied wine has a tinge of amber in the color and offers lean red-cherry and cider flavors.

name: Shokrian Vineyard 2017 Rue Dauphine Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Black raspberry, wet clay and rose petal aromas converge on the dynamic nose of this brisk bottling from a vineyard near Los Alamos. The wine is fresh, snappy and lively on the sip, where an energetic tension delivers bright, easy-to-like flavors of red currant and white pepper.

name: Sean W. McBride 2017 The China Block Filigreen Farm Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This complex, flavorful and earthy-tasting wine offers robust aromas of turned earth and barnyard that lead to rich, ripe red and black cherry and a touch of rhubarb on the palate. It's moderately and appropriately tannic and has good acidity to give the texture a good bite.

name: Sean W. McBride 2017 Old Saint George Filigreen Farm Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Earthy and exotic, this full-bodied wine is a bit of a throwback, showing barnyard and leather aromas and savory earthy flavors surrounding ripe cherry notes. Quite nice for people who appreciate some funkiness.

name: Sean Minor 2017 Sangiacomo Roberts Road Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tangy orange peel, strawberry and raspberry highlight a fresh, delicate structure in this wine. Integrated tannins and sprinklings of cardamom and cinnamon add length to the full-bodied palate.

name: Seagrape 2017 Jump Up Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Very tangy aromas of pomegranate and plum extract meet with tarragon on the nose of this bottling. Rusty, sanguine flavors meld with tangy strawberry on the slightly sour palate.

name: Seabold Cellars 2017 Pelio East Ridge Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: A lighter yet tremendously dynamic style of Pinot Noir, this bottling begins with hibiscus, pomegranate, wild thyme, bay leaf and a hint of eucalyptus on the nose. Those forest-floor elements carry into the palate, where crisp raspberry flavors are enhanced by juniper and pine-needle freshness.

name: Sea Smoke 2017 Ten Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Candied cherry and darker berry aromas meet with star anise, crumple purple flowers and crushed slate on the intoxicating nose of this bottling from the estate biodynamically farmed monopole vineyard. Intense acidity and lavish tannins make for a simultaneously bright and rich expression on the palate, where black cherry and black raspberry are lofted by cardamom and mace.

name: Sea Smoke 2017 Southing Monopole Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: This monopole bottling from the coveted biodynamic vineyard begins with lush, intense and inviting aromas of dark boysenberry paste, wild mint, star anise and shaved nutmeg. The palate is bright with acidity yet redolent with dark-berry and black-currant flavors that give way to cola, mace and more exotic spices toward the finish.

name: Sea Slopes–Fort Ross Winery 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This red offers outstanding value for the quality: a balanced, textured and varietally satisfying expression of coastal coolness and acidity. Earthy flavors of tea, cola, black licorice and dried herb provide an exoticism amidst elegant layers of orange peel and cranberry.

name: Sculpterra 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Paso Robles)
review: The nose on this wine is quite compelling, showing dried cherry, hibiscus, lavender, roasted pork and rose-hip aromas. The palate lands with a bit of sweetness, offering dark-cherry and strawberry flavors with a pinch of nutmeg.

name: Schug 2017 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Brooding in black cherry, cranberry and currant, this is a selection of certain blocks planted to a variety of clones. Herbaceous earth, toasted oak and supple tannin round out the experience of elegance and length.

name: Scheid 2017 Doctor's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Quite light in the glass, this single-vineyard expression offers aromas of wild cherry, dried sage, tarragon, crushed stone and root beer on the nose. The palate is ripe yet balanced, with flavors of cherry juice, vanilla bean, star anise and dried ginger.

name: Scar of the Sea 2017 Seven Leagues Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Elegant aromas of dried sagebrush, bright cranberry, raspberry paste and hibiscus are immensely fresh on the nose of this bottling. There is a compelling herbal character to the palate, from bay leaf to thyme and sagebrush, as well as wild fruit flavors of strawberry, cranberry and roasted plum.

name: Scar of the Sea 2017 Block Q Bien Nacido Vineyard Old Vine Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
designation: Block Q Bien Nacido Vineyard Old Vine
review: Coming from a block planted in 1973, this wine begins with lush layers of baked alpine strawberry, cinnamon and clove on the French toast spice-laden nose. The sip is taut in texture, delivering complex and deep flavors of strawberry, sagebrush, rose petal and bay leaf oil in unending waves of expression.

name: Sarah's Vineyard 2017 Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Clara Valley)
review: Tart and tangy aromas of berry and orange rind are quite prominent on the nose of this bottling. That tartness extends to the sip, where pomegranate and cranberry-skin flavors are lifted with pinches of baking spice.

name: Sarah's Vineyard 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Santa Clara Valley)
review: Concentrated aromas of caramel and dark plum meet with strong oak on the nose of this bottling. The wine lands quite thickly on the palate, where ripe and dried red fruits are wrapped in strong oak tones of caramel and vanilla.

name: Santori 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Herbal and thick, this wine lacks fruit and is relatively flat in structure and texture.

name: Sanford 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Crisp aromas of black cherry sorbet, slate and wet earth show on the nose of this appellation blend, which is sure to feature mostly if not all estate fruit from this historic producer. There's a great mineral streak on the palate, where concrete and crushed stone flavors frame the dark cranberry core.

name: Sandhi 2017 Sanford & Benedict Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Quite light in the glass, this bottling by Rajat Parr and Sashi Moorman explores the green and deeply savory side of Pinot Noir. Aromas of green peppercorns, freshly plucked green sage and tart raspberry lead into a palate awash with wild chaparral herbs and underripe red-fruit flavors, finishing on lingering tea notes. Some will love it, many will be perplexed by it.

name: Samsara 2017 Cargasacchi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Light in the glass, this bottling from a coveted vineyard offers white-pepper, wild peppercorns and crunchy red-currant aromas on the nose. The palate is piquant, with spice and fruit, ranging from fresh wild strawberry to more dank herbal tones.

name: Samsara 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: This appellation blend offers aromas of cracked pepper, sagebrush, fennel frond and wild thyme on the savory nose. The palate brings flavors of black raspberry and roasted tomato spiced with abundant peppercorns.

name: Samsara 2017 Las Hermanas Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Loads of peppercorns, wild thyme, tobacco and pine-needle aromas ride across crisp raspberry fruit on the nose of this bottling by winemaker Matt Brady. Those herbal flavors dominate the palate as well, where wild chaparral tones meet with crunchy cranberry fruit.

name: Samsara 2017 Rancho La Vina Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dried cranberry and wild oregano and thyme aromas show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression from the southern edge of the appellation. Strong black-raspberry flavors meet with black tea, cardamom and more exotic Indian spices on the palate.

name: Samsara 2017 Kessler-Haak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Candied red-cherry and raspberry-jam aromas meet with chaparral, sagebrush and cracked pepper touches on the nose of this bottling. It's fairly light in texture on the palate, where tangy raspberry and red-plum-skin flavors ride strong acidity.

name: Samsara 2017 Lotus Reserve Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Intriguingly savory lines of sandalwood and wild sage elevate the dark raspberry and purple-flower aromas on the nose of this reserve bottling. Dried strawberry flavors, leather and roasted game flavors show on the sip, which remains tightly wound with more energy ready to emerge.

name: Ryan Cochrane 2017 Solomon Hills Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Sanguine aromas of iron meet with roses, carnations, pomegranates and peppercorns on the earthy, spicy nose of this bottling by the late vintner, who died in January 2019. Intriguing lines of Montmorency cherry and tart pomegranate converse with bay leaf and sansho pepper on the palate.

name: Ryan Cochrane 2017 Fiddlestix Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Slightly sour pomegranate but also baked cherry aromas meet with clove dust on the nose of this bottling. Bright red-currant flavors are enhanced by mace and star anise elements on the palate.

name: Ryan Cochrane 2017 La Encantada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: The aromas of black plum, pomegranate, carnation, mace and sumac are dense on the nose of this single-vineyard bottling. The palate zips with acidity and stays light on its toes, delivering flavors of black raspberry, rose petal and minty tea.

name: Rusack 2017 Santa Catalina Island Vineyards Pinot Noir (California)
review: Herby tones of eucalyptus and wild herbs meet with black-plum skin and rustic berries on the nose of this always compelling bottling from Catalina Island. That herbal complexity carries to the palate as well, where dark red-berry flavors are lifted by minty chaparral spices.

name: Roth 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Made in a fleshy, opulent style, this hearty red is juicy and lively in dense black cherry and raspberry. Black tea and forest floor provide contrast around broad tannins and generous toasted oak.

name: Ron Rubin 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This light wine tastes of thick, candied black cherry and savory black tea, with a focused thread of acidity throughout. Clove and nutmeg accent the fruit around textured, well-integrated tannin and oak.

name: Reeve 2017 Wendling Vineyard North Slope Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Crisp and bright acidity underscores tart and tangy red- and black-berry fruit in this biodynamically farmed wine. Focused and fresh, it offers earthy elements of stone and crushed rock alongside forest floor and wild mushroom, presenting a feral quality on the finish.

name: Reeve 2017 Rice-Spivak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Crisp red fruit, nuances of black tea and earth are delicately woven around a grippy structure and firm tannin in this memorable, elegant wine. From a true coastal site, it floats within layers of blood orange and wild strawberry, finishing in a dusting of cardamom and white pepper.

name: Reeve 2017 Mirama Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Layered and velvety, this is a bright, expansive wine, with depths of crisp red and orange fruit. It blends together grapes from three marquee sites: Rice-Spivak, English Hill and Thorn Ridge, giving grip and gravelly texture around endlessly energetic acidity.

name: Reeve 2017 Kiser Vineyard Suitcase Block Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: High toned in blueberry, raspberry and wild strawberry, this wine is tart, tangy and well developed, its acidity racy and full of energy. From one of the coldest sites in the appellation, it imparts complex layers of black tea, cardamom and penny royal, crisp and textured throughout.

name: Red Car 2017 Heaven and Earth Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Cool climate in style and ripeness, this balanced red is fruity and savory, with a nice arrangement between crunchy pomegranate, cherry and strawberry alongside cardamom and black tea. Light bodied, it retains acidity through a lengthy finish.

name: Reata 2017 El Diablo Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Herbal, firm in tannin and tangy in orange and strawberry, this is a complex, intriguing and full-bodied red wine. It has length and breadth across the midpalate and underlying acidity that keeps it fresh and lively in the glass.

name: Reata 2017 Reata Las Brisas Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This wine is thick in feel, with tones of herbs and forest floor masked by oak. It is a bit simplistic in its approach.

name: Read Holland 2017 Deep End Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Concentrated black-fruit flavors just on the edge of full ripeness give this full-bodied wine a vivid, bracing focus. A firm, lightly tannic texture lifts the black cherry, sour cherry and black tea nuances.

name: Rancho Sisquoc 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Quite light in the glass, this bottling from a historic ranch begins with aromas of light cherry, singed raspberry and potpourri. The palate combines dried cherry, cranberry and orange rind, finishing with a zing of white pepper.

name: Ramey 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is sourced from a selection of different vineyards, including Bucher along Westside Road. Earthy funk underlies high-toned red cherry, rhubarb and strawberry, threading the needle between richness and crispness. A salty savory finish tops things off.

name: Prodigal 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tangy black-raspberry flavors lean toward a balsamic tone while picking up light sage and tarragon on the nose of this bottling from Quinta Santa Rosa Vineyard. The palate combines tart black plums and lavender with cardamom spice.

name: Precision Wine 2017 Technique Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earthy, herbal and savory in black tea, this is a full-bodied and concentrated wine offering good value and juicy flavors of cranberry and blood orange. It finishes awash in cinnamon.

name: Potek 2017 Sanford & Benedict Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Intense aromas of black raspberry, cracked black pepper, bay leaf and toasty wood make for an herbal and savory spin on this historic vineyard. The peppery spices carry the palate, with green sage and white pepper sprinkled across the mulberry and cranberry flavors.

name: Portalupi 2017 Dolinsek Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: A highly ripe, robust wine, this red tastes earthy and is jammy in strawberry and dark cherry, with an undeniable earthiness and full-figured tannin.

name: Pisoni 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Dense and concentrated on the nose, this iconic bottling shows intense aromas of black cherry, sage, clove and cardamom. The tannins are fairly rigid at this young age, giving structure to the rich fruit and spice tones that are seamlessly integrated. Drink 2021–2042.

name: Piro 2017 Runway Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Dried cranberry and cherry meet with a rusty minerality on the nose of this bottling, from a newer vineyard adjacent to Bien Nacido. The hearty palate combines rich wild cherries with sage, orange-rind and roast pork flavors.

name: Piro 2017 Presqu'ile Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Blistered tomato, sage and wild cranberry aromas take on a sanguine tone of blood sausage on the nose of this very cool-climate take on Pinot Noir. The palate is an herbal array of peppercorn and wild mint, leading into layers of lavender, Sichuan pepper and scorched cranberry.

name: Piro 2017 Points West Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Crisp and lively aromas of pomegranate and cherry meet with bay leaf, pine needle and green cardamom on the nose of this blend of the Runway and Presqu'ile vineyards. The palate snaps with eucalyptus and a damp pine-menthol character, leading into bright and brisk raspberry flavors.

name: Phillips Hill 2017 Boontling Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Elegance and depth come together beautifully in this posh, well-balanced and luxuriously ripe-tasting wine. Gorgeous maraschino cherry and sweet plum flavors meet subtle tannins and good acidity on the palate, giving a sunny impression that's hard to resist.

name: Phillips Hill 2017 Day Ranch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: An enticing, gripping, chalk and mineral vein adds verve and complexity to this quietly concentrated wine. It is medium to full in feel, layered with fine-grained tannins and intricate nuances of cherry and raspberry, all supported by well-integrated oak spices and a subtle tannin structure that will help it age. Best through 2027.

name: Phillips Hill 2017 Oppenlander Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: This smooth, silky wine is polished and well behaved, from the subtle cherry and vanilla aromas to the sleek texture that carries its mild oak and elegant fruit flavors through a lingering finish. The vineyard is on a high ridge between Anderson Valley and the Pacific Coast.

name: Phillips Hill 2017 Roma's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This medium-bodied wine from a distinctive single vineyard is exciting for its focus and purity. It offers intense, concentrated, vibrant and exotic black-fruit flavors while supporting them with a near-perfect balance of mild tannins and slightly bracing acidity.

name: Peter Paul Wines 2017 Mille Freres Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This wine is intensely herbal and robustly ripe, a contradiction that gives it a disjointed sense of cohesion. Oak and tannin present as a touch astringent on the finish.

name: Peter Paul Wines 2017 Live Free or Die Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This wine opens in earthy forest floor and robust oak, the tannins strong and prominent on the palate. Intensely ripe, the fruit is elusive, opting for more of a savory character and texture.

name: Pence 2017 Fugio Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Quite gamy on the nose, with hints of raw meat and wild herbs, this bottling also offers wild red-currant, bay leaf and peppercorn aromas. The palate is complex and wild, with flavors of pepper-crusted beef served under a orange-rind and cranberry compote and dusted with thyme and tobacco leaf.

name: Pence 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Savory aromas of bay leaf, juniper and rosemary play against the wild berry, sumac and dried mulberry aromas on the nose of this herbaceous bottling. The savory and herbal streak consumes the palate as well, where tart cranberry fruit is decorated with fennel pollen, oregano and sanguine flavors.

name: Pence 2017 Crate Estate Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Earthy herbs, dried berries, bay leaf and caramel cream aromas show on the nose of this bottling. Cranberry-juice and rose-petal flavors meet with juniper and stewed berries on the palate.

name: Pence 2017 Unum Estate Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: The nose of this bottling from the estate's first block ever planted is playful and expressive, offering aromas of cranberry, raspberry, bay leaf, juniper and Indian spice. The underripe fruit tones explode on the palate alongside compelling pine needle and wild herb qualities.

name: Peltier 2017 Barrel Aged Cosumnes River Pinot Noir (Lodi)
review: This well-built, medium-bodied wine has a good, grippy texture to balance ample flavors of grilled plums, cinnamon and clove while leaving the palate clean for the next bite of food.

name: Peake Ranch 2017 Peake Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Full aromas of roasted strawberry, hickory, roasting herbs and a hint of game show on the nose of this estate bottling from a newer brand in the eastern Sta. Rita Hills. There is prominent, plush and savory oak influence on the palate, where rich red fruits, vanilla, ginger and more baking-spice flavors arise.

name: Peake Ranch 2017 John Sebastiano Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Hickory smoke, red cherries and vanilla aromas show on the nose of this bottling from one of the many vineyards owned by proprietor John Wagner. The red-cherry flavor on the palate is simultaneously fresh and dried, while star anise, vanilla-bean and more baking-spice flavors carry into the finish.

name: Peake Ranch 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Cinnamon, sumac, hibiscus, rose petal and pomegranate are dusted with a hint of wild thyme on the nose of this appellation blend. There is compelling tension to the sip, where hickory smoke flavors decorate the cranberry and pomegranate fruit.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Pisoni Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: This is a hearty, smoky and rather burly wine from this legendary vineyard, with dense, deep and savory aromas of campfire-grilled meats, charred blood and intense blackberry. The feral smoke element weaves through the sip, where scorched cherry and nutmeg flavors fill in the gaps.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Alder Springs Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: This wine is a bit tight in texture, stiffened by firm tannins typical of the vineyard that wrap around vivid red- and black-cherry flavors. While full bodied, it's not at all fat, but offers a feeling of concentration held slightly in check. Best from 2023.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Moses-Hall Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This wine is juicy and rich, with savory dried herb and toasted oak figuring into the mix. With Carneros' classic funky earthiness, it is tart and edgy, with a thread of nutmeg and clove.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Jenkins Ranch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A perennial standout in the producer's line-up of vineyard-designates, this wine is lovely, complex and textured. Black tea, forest floor, clove and rhubarb keep it savory and earthy in the glass, while robust and refined tannin, a length of white pepper are there at its finish.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Goldrock Ridge Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a far, cool and extremely coastal site near Annapolis, this wine is sturdy and strong in tannin, weighty and textured. A core of jammy blueberry and black cherry find contrast in black tea, crushed rock and persistent acidity, lengthy and delicious.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Gap's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From the great Petaluma Gap site, long a source for the producer, this vintage shows a wealth of juicy black fruit alongside crunchy cranberry and currant. Rounded and textured in velvety tannins, it finishes with fistfuls of clove, nutmeg and toasted oak.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Chenoweth Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a great site farmed by Charlie Chenoweth, this is a grippy, full-bodied and robust red wine that's textured and tremendously complex. Rich flavors of black cherry and raspberry are complemented by deft, savory tones of black tea and forest floor that accentuate the long finish.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Burnside Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earthy forest and buzzy acidity open this wine on the nose and palate, easing into a broad texture of velvety richness and brooding tannin. Black tea, cherry and a touch of cola combine around the savory undertones and lasting freshness.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Little Boot Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earthy in grape stem, tree bark and forest, this complex, confounding and memorable wine is high toned in fresh acidity. The structure cradles tart, tangy waves of citrus, cranberry and pomegranate—a depth of flavor that is in balance and finishes with grace.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Bootlegger's Hill Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is marked by big, bold flavor and grippy tannin delivering an inviting intensity that delights and confounds. Black cherry, plum and wild strawberry give it wide appeal and power, with an earthiness that melds within with ease and length.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Hyde Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Stewed black fruit fills a dense texture-packed core of tannin and pronounced oak in this exuberant wine, with moderately structured acidity lending a balanced approach. Blue fruit and a touch of vanilla meet the palate head-on, showing a lifted intensity and noticeable length.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Sanchietti Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Tart raspberry, rhubarb and cherry star in this vineyard-designate, a well-integrated, supple and complex celebration of the grape. Light in baking spice and earthy forest, it offers length and breadth across the velvety palate.

name: Patz & Hall 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A savory edge of sage and dried herb combines with richer flavors of berries and cola. Textured tannins and bright acidity lend support to this balanced wine from a cool-climate appellation.

name: Parducci 2017 Small Lot Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: This wine is big and brawny, offering vibrant cherry-fruit flavors and a firm texture underlain by good acidity and moderate tannins. It will pair up nicely with rich proteins.

name: Paradise Springs 2017 Founders Block Sanford & Benedict Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dried flower petals, roasted red fruit and leather aromas show on the nose of this bottling from old vines at this historic vineyard. Those vines carry an entrenched elegance, which shows on the palate's raspberry, hibiscus and leather flavors, all wrapped up in simmering acidity.

name: Paradise Springs 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tightly woven black-raspberry aromas meet with dank earth and shiitake aromas on the nose of this appellation blend. Dark and dried fruit flavors show on the palate alongside more earthy mushrooms and a flash of cola spice.

name: Paradise Springs 2017 Sanford & Benedict Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Strong aromas of dark mulberry are layered with charred wood, crushed slate, wet gravel, dried mushrooms and many more earthy, rocky elements on the nose of this bottling from a historic vineyard. The palate begins with roasted fruit before leading into baked earth, dried oregano and crushed stone flavors.

name: Paradise Ridge 2017 Walter's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is reductive and aromatic in oak, with robust underlying power. Flavors range from dark cherry and pomegranate to black tea and cola, with specks of vanilla from the wood.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 777 Clones Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is moderately structured and jammy in dark-cherry fruit. A hint of cola adds to a richness on the palate.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Pommard Clones Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Silky smooth in red and orange fruit, this clone-specific red is medium bodied and wrapped in toasted oak, showing a touch of cola and black tea.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Rhubarb, pomegranate and strawberry combine for a velvety expression of the variety in a warm vintage. Edgy in stemmy complexity and tones of black tea, it is light in approach and power, made in a graceful style.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Thin and relatively light bodied, this wine is also lightly structured, with nuanced flavors of wild strawberry and pungent, savory earth. Forest floor and tea highlight the finish.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Campbell Ranch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Light in baking spice, this wine has firm tannins that wrap around juicy red- and black-cherry flavors, with underlying aspects of forest floor and earth. Richly layered, it has soft, full-bodied concentration and impressive length.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Peters Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earthy and sultry in forest floor and clove, this wine is juicy, lengthy and structured, with layers upon layers of black cherry and strawberry. Full bodied and robustly concentrated, it lasts in deep flavor and toasted oak.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Leras Family Vineyards Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Light in color and reductive on the nose, this wine is pronounced in oak. The flavors rely on a mix of dark cherry, strawberry and herbal earthiness, finishing fresh and focused.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Nunes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Black-cherry flavors take on a syrupy concentration in this full-figured, lightly spiced vineyard-designate. Bold flavor is lifted by fresh, persistent acidity that softens the intensity and adds to the wine's length.

name: Papapietro Perry 2017 Charles Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This wine has a mysterious slant to the flavors, offering intriguing notes of mushroom, moss and forest floor along with rich black plum. On the finish, a touch of baking spice follows through. This kind of complexity helps it stand out from the crowd, and the medium to full body and velvety tannins make it easy to sip.

name: Pali 2017 Rancho la Vina Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dark black-plum, mossy earth, toasted wood and dried porcini aromas show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Violet and nutmeg flavors decorate the palate, where a strong black-plum-sauce element is most prominent.

name: Pali 2017 Pommard Block 2 Pali Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Light pomegranate and baked plum aromas show on the shy nose of this clonal selection. Flavors of earthy rust, dried herbs, cracked pepper and plum come through on the palate.

name: Pali 2017 Pali Vineyard Reserve Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Aromas of cola, clove, nutmeg, cedar and black plum show on the nose of this reserve bottling, which included six barrels of 100% new French oak. Rich and deep flavors of black plum and black raspberry meet with white pepper and strong acidity on the palate.

name: Pali 2017 Pali Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Rich blackberry-jam and black-plum aromas are enhanced by baking spice on the concentrated nose of this bottling. The palate offers black-raspberry-jam flavors along with cardamom cream and clove, proving tangy and spicy.

name: Pali 2017 John Sebastiano Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Ripe aromas of cherry compote meet with clove and nutmeg on the nose of this bottling. The mouthfeel is rich and hearty, but the palate also presents snappy flavors of black raspberry, black-plum skin and mace on the finish.

name: Pali 2017 Fiddlestix Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Red cherry compote, earthy herbs and a hint of oak show on the nose of this bottling. The medium-bodied palate offers delicious cherry, raspberry-sorbet and red-currant jam flavors, with a touch of wild herbs on a very soft mouthfeel.

name: Pali 2017 Radian Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Roasted cherry and boysenberry aromas meet with an earthy iodine and rust touch on the nose of this bottling. The palate combines dried thyme and oregano against grilled black-plum flavors, and there's a lingering hint of tobacco toward the finish.

name: Odonata 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Red-cherry, roasted sagebrush and campfire aromas show on the nose of this appellation blend. There's a strong amount of oak on the smoky palate, where solid acidity upholds flavors of red fruit, vanilla and baking spice.

name: Odonata 2017 Falcon Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Dark cherry aromas meet with more mineral-driven hints of rust and wild herb-laced wet earth elements on the nose of this single-vineyard expression—a property that's farmed entirely by this winery. Blistered cherries and roasted red plums converge with tarragon, mace and nutmeg on the palate, which finishes with toasty tones.

name: Niner 2017 JR Reserve Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Light red berry and crushed potpourri aromas show on the subtle nose of this reserve bottling. Smooth tannins shape the sip, where baked boysenberrry, cardamom and cinnamon flavors lead into a long vanilla-laced finish.

name: Neely 2017 Upper Picnic Block Spring Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
designation: Upper Picnic Block Spring Ridge Vineyard
review: Crisp raspberry aromas meet with a hint of wet earth and subdued herbs on the nose of this block-designated bottling. There's buoyant acidity on the sip, where black-raspberry, oak and mushroom flavors converge.

name: Neely 2017 Lost Road Spring Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Tart tomato and underripe red fruits meet with grape-seed oil on the nose of this bottling. The palate shows damp herb and a light berry-compote flavors.

name: Neely 2017 Hidden Block Spring Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Intense boysenberry, anise, clove and nutmeg aromas converge for a spicy and fresh nose on this block-designated bottling. That dark fruit continues on the palate, lifted by lively acidity and elegant brushes of dried purple flower.

name: Neely 2017 Picnic Block Spring Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Light boysenberry and black plum join floral touches of dried purple flowers on the nose of this bottling. Smashed berry and grape-seed flavors meet with a bit of nuts, dried lilac and dark rose petals on the nose.

name: Murphy-Goode 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Fresh tasting and well balanced, this medium-bodied wine is pleasant and enjoyable for its mulled cherry and strawberry notes, rounded mouthfeel and a nip of tannin to firm up the texture.

name: Mount Eden Vineyards 2017 The Tanaka Project Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: This bottling is a personal one for the Patterson family, dedicated to funding suicide prevention after the loss of their friend Ron Tanaka. The wine offers hearty aromas of blistered plum, funky mushroom and hints of dried seaweed and iodine on the nose. The palate also reflects those savory seashore elements along with touches of dried cranberry and mulberry touches. It's built for aging, so drink now through 2032.

name: Mount Eden Vineyards 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: There's intense pine and redwood spice to the nose and palate of this bottling, which begins with aromas of cooked cranberry, vanilla and nutmeg. There's a splash of acid on the tip of the sip, where smoked strawberry and grilled berry flavors meet with oregano and singed oak smoke.

name: Morgan 2017 Double L Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of crushed rosebuds and carnations mix with intense raspberry and a touch of eucalyptus on the nose of this single-vineyard expression that's made from organic grapes. It's fresh and lively on the palate, with a pristine raspberry flavors that's enhanced by touches of wild thyme and sage.

name: Mon Frere 2017 Vintner's Selection Pinot Noir (California)
review: Fruity and well structured, this affordable wine offers generous black-cherry and baking-spice flavors and an appetizing texture that lightly grips the palate. Rhubarb and tea notes come out with further time in the glass.

name: Miro 2017 Reserve Hogan's Run Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Herbal and minty, this wine has a juicy middle of robust strawberry and dark cherry. The tannin profile is soft and well integrated, leading to a simple finish.

name: Minus Tide 2017 Mariah Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)
review: Distinctive floral and rosemary aromas dress up this medium-bodied and soft-textured wine, making it fragrant and likeable but atypical, offering light and herbaceous-leaning fruit flavors underneath.

name: Miner 2017 Garys' Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: This winery is taking a lighter spin on the appellation, and this bottling works particularly well. A rusty shade in the glass, this wine offers aromas of pomegranate, cherry, wild mint and sage on the nose. The palate follows in line, combining crumpled sagebrush and thyme flavors with macerated cranberry and cherry, finishing on pops of cinnamon and clove.

name: Miner 2017 Sierra Mar Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Quite light in the glass, particularly for this appellation, this bottling begins with aromas of pomegranate syrup, wet gravel, tea leaves and herbs on the nose. There's a sour cherry kick to the palate, where dried tarragon and loamy earth qualities lead into a French toast spice finish.

name: Migration 2017 Dutton Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From sites farmed by the Duttons in both Green Valley and Sebastopol Hills, this balanced red is savory and earthy in style, with highlights of forest floor, black tea and pronounced tannin. The rich fruit is a mix of blackberry, cherry and strawberry that glide along a seamless texture, ending bright and light.

name: Migration 2017 Running Creek Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a block and barrel selection wine, sourced from the producer's 90-acre, dry-farmed estate. Soft layers of orange, strawberry and cherry are textured and well integrated, the tannins sturdy but not obtrusive. Oak underscores its intensity, adding a touch of vanilla.

name: Migration 2017 Drum Canyon Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Intense aromas of candied Bing cherry are leveled by a powerful minerality on the nose of this bottling, where wet, crushed slate scents are prominent. Pristine red-fruit flavors are cut by French toast spice and framed by tightly woven tannins that will endure for ages. Drink now through 2032.

name: Migration 2017 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Quite light in the glass, this single-vineyard expression mixes cherry with turned earth, crushed slate and hints of smoke on the nose. There is excellent structure on the palate, where crisp pomegranate meets with underlying elements of game, delicate dried herbs and more smoke. It is complex and savory yet refreshing enough to enjoy all day.

name: Migration 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is an earthy, dense wine made from a range of expertly farmed vineyards like Running Creek. Black tea, cardamom and forest give it a savory character that's appealing and expansive on the palate, showing balanced richness and length.

name: Michael Pozzan 2017 Giapoza Pinot Noir (California)
review: Quite ripe tasting and showing early maturity, this full-bodied wine offers dried plum and raisin flavors, a rich texture and full body.

name: Mi Sueño 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a hearty red wine, full bodied and opulent in style, with touches of reduction, plenty of oak and Cabernet-like intensity. Dark, brooding flavors of dark cherry and plum ride an undercurrent of black tea and mocha, finishing in vanilla oak.

name: Metzker Family Estates 2017 Terra de Promissio Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Thick, stony and edgy in dried herb and ash, this wine is robustly grainy and textured, with a density of black fruit and tannin. Dark cherry and berry mingle around a richness of cola and caramel.

name: Metzker Family Estates 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Lifted red fruit on the nose is complemented by citrus, oak and graham cracker in this robust, full-bodied wine. Supple tannins lend ample support to give it staying power.

name: Metz Road 2017 Riverview Vineyard Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: From a vineyard on the eastside of the Salinas River, this bottling begins with aromas of baked cherry, smoked meats and cardamom dust. It's a tad thin on the palate, but offers pleasing flavors of cranberry, smoked beef and hibiscus.

name: Merryvale 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Herbal and spicy, this wine is high in acidity and high-toned flavors of cranberry, rhubarb and strawberry. Medium bodied, it retains powerful tannin and oak.

name: Merry Edwards 2017 Cuvée Charles Li Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: A special wine made to celebrate the birth of Edwards' second grandchild, born in the year of the fire rooster, this is a barrel selection from Meredith Estate. Baked cherry, fig and anise combine for a richly layered sensation of velvety smoothness and ample structure, with the taste of dark chocolate nipping around the edges. Aromas of rose petal persist on the nose. This will do well in the cellar; enjoy best from 2022–2027.

name: Merry Edwards 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This blend of multiple vineyards impresses in its breadth and depth, offering creamy softness and robust yet supple tannin. Toasted oak, nutmeg, clove and brooding black fruit combine effortlessly along a medium-bodied thread of ripeness and weight, finishing in a measured richness of chocolate.

name: Merry Edwards 2017 Meredith Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This was Edwards' first estate planting, begun in 1998. It shows incredible attention to detail and depth of site, opening with memorable floral, earthy and mineral-laden characters. Robust in structure, the dense, youthful power of the wine is robust in an intensity of plum, dark cherry and an intermixed swirl of vanilla and chocolate. It finishes on white pepper and will age beautifully. Enjoy from 2025–2030.

name: Merry Edwards 2017 Klopp Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Wild in pomegranate, strawberry and brambly blackberry, this longstanding vineyard-designate is deep and rich, with a density of earthiness. Thick tannins support the fruit and building elements of dried herb and lavender, allowing room for the acidity to balance its overall luxury.

name: Merry Edwards 2017 Flax Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a Middle Reach site along Westside Road, this robust, earthy wine is strong in firm tannin and overall structure. Earthy and bright in underlying acidity, it dazzles in dark, rich flavors of strawberry, blueberry and dried herb, a whiff of white pepper lurking. A finishing touch of dark chocolate leaves one wanting more.

name: Melville 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Crisp aromas of raspberry sorbet and cherry-mint glaze meet with a touch of green herbs and menthol on the nose of this bottling, which enlivens the senses. Fresh strawberry and blistered tomato flavors arise on the sip, backed by eucalyptus and pine-needle touches, making for a glass that's guzzled quickly.

name: Melville 2017 Anna's Block Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: White-pepper and sour cherry aromas meet with mint balm on the fresh, lively and strongly stem-influenced nose of this bottling. It's incredibly alive on the palate, with tons of eucalyptus, sagebrush and potting-soil flavors, set against a vibrant raspberry and light cherry backdrop. The buzz of Sichuan peppercorns coats the finish.

name: Melville 2017 Block M Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Loads of bay leaf, wild chaparral shrubs, oak forest floor, green sage and sansho pepper play against a brisk raspberry backdrop on the nose of this lively bottling. Lush, delicious and vivid flavors of red fruit meet with pine needle and eucalyptus on the palate, which is simultaneously hedonistic and intellectual.

name: Melville 2017 Sandy's Block Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Intensely candied Bing cherry aromas meet with strong herbal influence, from bay leaf to green peppercorns, on the nose of this fruity spice bomb. The palate explodes with flavors of raspberry and ripe cherry, as eucalyptus-tea tones linger deep into the finish.

name: McIntyre Vineyards 2017 McIntyre Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Fresh and bright black-raspberry aromas meet with a richer black-cherry streak as well as sagebrush and crushed slate on the nose of this bottling by regional pioneer Steve McIntyre. It sizzles on the palate with spicy root beer and black-cherry flavors, undercut by a pencil-lead minerality.

name: McIntyre Vineyards 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: The Santa Lucia Highlands is known for ripeness and power, yet this is a delicate, floral-enhanced bottling. Aromas of rich strawberry are cut by wet slate, gravel and rose petals, while the fresh, flavorful palate combines hibiscus cream, raspberry sorbet and white pepper.

name: McHenry 2017 Swan Clone Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Very light in the glass, this clonal selection, which the winery only pursues in certain vintages, begins with fresh aromas of raspberry, dark hibiscus, wild mint and rusty blood. A cranberry and strawberry tone grabs hold of the palate, yet the wild thyme, marjoram, tea-leaf and rose-petal flavors are what makes it stand out.

name: McHenry 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Focused aromas of cherry and raspberry juice meet with earthy thyme, light sage and white pepper on the nose of this bottling from a small family vineyard in Bonny Doon. There's great depth to the sip, where dark-raspberry and boysenberry flavors are lifted by mace and dried ginger spices. High acid and a grippy texture round out the experience.

name: Maryetta 2017 Black Hoop Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This wine is spicy in flavor, lively and well balanced, and shows an excellent interplay between ripe fruit and judicious oak flavors. Subtle toasty, cinnamon aromas lead to red cherry and black cherry on the palate, braced by subtle tannins and acidity.

name: Maryetta 2017 Valenti Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)
review: Peppery oaky smoky aromas and flavors grab all the attention in this full-bodied, firm-textured wine. It comes off a bit tough and woody, and needs a rich, heavy dish to pair with.

name: Martinelli 2017 Bella Vigna Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is an earthy and fresh varietal wine, with a surprising level of density and weight. Dark berry fruit and baking spice combine across a seamless palate of integrated tannin and oak, ending with a jammy richness that satisfies.

name: Martin Ray 2017 Coast Grade Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Carnation, cranberry, licorice and charred wood aromas converge on the nose of this bottling from a vineyard that overlooks the Pacific Ocean. The palate is complex with stewed strawberry, orange peel and a pinch of herbs.

name: Martin Ranch 2017 Therese Vineyards Griva Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)
review: Tangy aromas of smashed plums, roasted wood, turned earth and slight game show on the nose of this bottling. There is immense wood spice on the palate, where slightly sour fruit flavors of red plum and wild berry are wrapped in cedar smoke.

name: Martin Ranch 2017 Therese Vineyards Lester Family Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Baked cherry, earthy rust, wild-tea and sage-oil aromas show on the nose of this bottling. It's cohesive and rich on the palate, where soft tannins deliver boysenberry, plum-skin and cracked pepper flavors.

name: Martin Ranch 2017 J.D. Hurley R.E.D. Vineyards Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Extracted and stewed blackberry-jam aromas meet with white pepper and oak on the nose of this bottling. It's soft and smooth on the sip, but with a powerful flavor of cedar and oak, which frame the blackberry, clove and nutmeg flavors.

name: Martin Ranch 2017 J.D. Hurley Red Vineyards Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Cooked cherries and strawberries meet with light clove and cardamom on the nose of this bottling. The palate is full of flavor, its core of red fruit and baking spice mostly dominated by a cedar and redwood spice.

name: Marnet 2017 Alder Springs Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Concentrated in flavor and a bit tight in texture, this well-balanced wine will open up with time. Cranberry and red-cherry aromas lead to tangy rhubarb and richer plum and woodsy flavors, while light tannins add good grip. Best from 2023.

name: MacRostie 2017 The Loch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tangy citrus is further boosted by tart, sharp acidity in this intensely full-bodied, ripe and boldly textured wine. The barrel imprint is strong, adding to the weight and breadth of the wine, which finishes in earth and black pepper.

name: MacRostie 2017 Cummings Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy strawberry, black tea and turned earth open into a midpalate of light, bright acidity, with a meaty underbelly of richness in this wine, sourced from a vineyard in the Santa Rosa Plains and farmed by Charlie Chenoweth. The finish hints of orange peel and cardamom.

name: MacRostie 2017 Olivet Lane Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a light, ethereal take on the variety, savory in forested earth, black tea and crispy red fruit. Made in a restrained, elegant style, it sings in acidity and energy, and has a subtle dusting of white pepper that is immensely complex.

name: MacRostie 2017 Thale's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: With a delicate floral nose met by cherry spice, this is a sumptuous and lovely red wine, fully lush in strawberry and black cherry. French oak wraps around the fruit effortlessly adding nuances of vanilla and baking spice.

name: MacRostie 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Creamy red fruit is complemented in a light, buzzy earthiness of tree bark, dried herb and mineral in this textured midweight wine. The appellation is well represented, the wine quietly rich in dark cherry, pomegranate and cola.

name: MacRostie 2017 Terra de Promissio Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From the famous Petaluma Gap site, this light-bodied, elegant wine is fresh and rich in baked strawberry, forest and dried herb. Lengthy in savory spice and black tea, it has structure to spare, with firm, supple tannin and integrated oak.

name: MacRostie 2017 Day Ranch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This nicely balanced wine shows all the good things about Pinot Noir in a compact, relatively light package. Red-cherry, oak-spice and tea-leaf aromas are followed by tangy cherry and cranberry flavors, energized by good acidity and mild tannins.

name: MacRostie 2017 Putnam Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a far Sonoma Coast vineyard near Annapolis, this is a tight, structured, high-toned and exotically savory red wine that's earthy and resiny. Balanced and textured, it opens slowly in the glass to allow just a touch of cranberry, wild strawberry and pomegranate to mix in.

name: MacRostie 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Floral, silky and succulent, this is a robust, full-bodied wine, expressive in blueberries and red-berry fruit. Dry tannins wrap around ripe layers of richness and texture and are seasoned lightly in dried herb and baking spice.

name: MacRostie 2017 Rodgers Creek Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: Tangy, edgy acidity underlies the buzzing core of this structured, high-toned wine from the new appellation. Stony mineral and crisp, crunchy red fruit defines its medium-bodied frame of complex tannin integration and graceful finish.

name: MacRostie 2017 Manzana Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Strawberry, raspberry and cola figure most prominently in this wine, which has a light touch of firm tannin and oak. Dark and brooding, it has a strong undertone of brushy forest and tea.

name: MacRostie 2017 Klopp Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This lovely, balanced wine from the great site is nuanced in rose, lavender and dark cherry-strawberry richness, the palate juicy and supple with generous tension and texture. Spicy forest and black tea mingle with ease amongst the core of fruit, the finish lengthy and graceful.

name: MacRostie 2017 Hellenthal Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
