review: From a high-elevation coastal site, this wine is dazzling in juicy red fruit, gentle spice and a great length and breadth of textured intensity. Toasted oak adds more spice and a note of dark chocolate, which combines well with the rich, rewarding core of berry.

name: MacPhail 2017 Mardikian Estate Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tightly focused tannin and oak form a formidable structure around well-defined flavors of black cherry, currant and forest in this full-bodied, densely concentrated wine. Thick, rich and complex, it packs a punch of intensity and memorable length and breadth.

name: MacPhail 2017 The Flyer Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tones of forest floor, rhubarb and cardamom lead this light-bodied, elegant red that's made from several sites then aged 11 months in French oak, 30% of it new. Delicate tannins and mild oak tones are lifted by buoyant edges of bright acidity.

name: MacPhail 2017 Wightman House Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Brooding, exotic aromas and deep, delicious flavors make this full-bodied, soft-textured wine fascinating and mysterious. Opulent but subtle spices and a rich foundation of black cherry, sour cherry and black tea make for complex flavors, while a mouthfilling presence is buoyed by mild tannins to complete the picture.

name: MacPhail 2017 Toulouse Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Packed with fruit flavors and laden with baking spices, this full-bodied and plush-textured wine is hard to resist. Loads of ripe black cherries and strawberries support generous dashes of cinnamon, vanilla and nutmeg on a soft, broad palate.

name: MacMurray Estate Vineyards 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Sweet oak and a juicy palate of cherry, cola and vanilla combine for a satisfyingly rich, approachable experience of soft, rounded tension and baking spice in this full-bodied, crowd-pleasing wine.

name: MacMurray Estate Vineyards 2017 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: This is a compelling wine for an extremely fair price, starting with pleasing aromas of red cherry compote and wood spice. The palate grows a bit more complex, offering well-integrated flavors of orange rind, red plum and turned earth, all framed by brisk acidity.

name: Lumen 2017 Sanford & Benedict Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Crystalline cranberry aromas meet with wet slate, rose petal and hibiscus juice on the sharp nose of this bottling from the historic vineyard. Zesty raspberry flavors meet with more slate minerality and wet gravel on the palate, which is fresh and vibrant in acidity.

name: Lula 2017 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This wine offers pure fruit flavors, a compelling sense of concentration and a firm structure for midterm aging. A core of black cherry is accented by rhubarb and forest-floor notes, and the finish brings in black tea. Firm, fine-grained tannins will allow it to cellar well through at least 2025.

name: Lucienne 2017 Lone Oak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of bay leaf and potting soil meet with dried porcini mushrooms and spicy red fruit on the nose of this bottling. The flavors are bright on the palate, with roasted red cherry, vanilla and oak holding court.

name: Lucienne 2017 Doctor's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of black cherry, clove, spiced cake and vanilla show on the nose of this bottling. The texture is soft and pillowy on the sip, where a splash of acidity underlies the hearty black-cherry and mocha-foam flavors.

name: Lucienne 2017 Smith Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of stewed cherries, root beer, licorice and fennel show on the nose of this single-vineyard bottling from the Hahn family. The palate is quite ripe with cherry and dried strawberry flavors, finishing with a prominent vanilla spice.

name: Lucienne 2017 Hook Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of Bing cherry, star anise, crushed rocks and a hint of oak show on the nose of this bottling from one of the Hahn family's beloved vineyards. The palate is powerful in flavors of Luxardo cherry, vanilla and French toast, proving rich and unctuous.

name: Lucas & Lewellen 2017 High 9 Goodchild Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Candied raspberry and roasted strawberry aromas meet with purple flowers and oak on the nose of this bottling. With 36% aged in new French oak, light strawberry and refreshing red-plum flavors rise above prominent oak tones on the palate.

name: Long Meadow Ranch 2017 Tanbark Mill Vineyard Perrygulch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Alluring baking-spice and strawberry-jam flavors make this medium-bodied, broad-textured wine almost irresistible. Hints of vanilla, cinnamon and nutmeg join with rich, relaxed fruit flavors an the velvety palate.

name: Long Meadow Ranch 2017 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This is a very good, warm, mouthfilling wine that supports mulled cherry, maple and ripe plum flavors with light tannins and medium body.

name: Lombardi 2017 Hill Justice Vineyard Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: Thick, rich and spicy, this vineyard-designate sings of forest floor and black tea, with a depth of baked black cherry. Big, firm tannin structure wraps around toasted oak and dusty rock.

name: Lombardi 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Barrel aged in French oak, 27% of it new, this is a thick, complex red wine from several cool-climate sites including Griffin's Lair and Spring Hill. Tangy red and orange fruit is lightly spiced in anise and cardamom, finishing in a complex grasp of grippy tannin.

name: Lloyd 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Baked cherry, smoke and clove aromas lead into a dusty minerality and light sagebrush tone on the complex nose of this bottling. The palate combines sage, baked cherry and cranberry flavors that finish on a smoked meat kick.

name: Line 39 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This is a solidly built, well-balanced wine that offers cranberry and red-cherry flavors, light tannins and a touch of crisp acidity. It's quite firm and snappy in texture, which yields an appetizing mouthfeel.

name: Light Horse 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Well made and easy to enjoy, this medium-bodied wine offers broadly fruity flavors with a bit of depth, and a touch of tannin to firm up the texture.

name: Lawer 2017 Betsy's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Knights Valley)
review: Juicy red cherry and strawberry highlight a densely tannic and full-bodied wine with strong elements of forest floor and damp earth.

name: LaRue 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Another cuvée made from top sites, this captures the beauty of three sites: 52% Rice-Spivak, 31% Thorn Ridge and 17% Emmaline Ann. Baking spice, brioche, rhubarb and orange flavors meet an undercurrent of salinity and ocean air, with thick tannin supporting the bright, racy acidity.

name: LaRue 2017 Rice-Spivak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tangy citrus undertones of lemon and grapefruit lift a midpalate that takes on darker, more savory elements of strawberry, dark cherry, clove, nutmeg and tea. Light and delicate on its feet, it holds steady on the lengthy palate in bright acidity and an elegant richness that provides ample mouthfeel and structure.

name: LaRue 2017 Thorn Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Lavender and rose make for a floral entry into this wine, which is exuberantly bright in racy cherry, blackberry and blood orange. Offering length and subtle richness, it is adorned further in clove, nutmeg and vanilla, with ample body weight to match the acidity.

name: LaRue 2017 Emmaline Ann Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a cool-climate site in a relatively warm year, this light red is bright and well structured, with lasting flavors of orange, cranberry and strawberry. It retains intense savory elements of forest, tobacco and cardamom, which complement and intrigue.

name: LaRue 2017 Coastlands Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is not a wine that owner and winemaker Katy Wilson makes every year. But it's worth noting when she does. This vintage is balanced in weight and style, with black tea, raspberry, strawberry and bay leaf mingling around strong tannin structure and notes of cardamom and vanilla.

name: Landmark 2017 Grand Detour Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tangy and crisp in red fruit, this wine offers ripeness and intensity around a hearty base of tannin and oak. Dried herb, clove and tobacco provide a savory underbelly with a hint of menthol on the finish.

name: Lafond 2017 SRH Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Aromas of stewed cranberry, nutmeg, dried roses and a hint of meat show on the nose of this bottling. There's plenty of vibrant, peppery acidity on the sip, as well as cracked pepper flavors, alongside baked raspberry and sour cherry.

name: Laetitia 2017 La Colline Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Roasted berries, dried sage and licorice aromas pop on the nose of this bottling from a hillside vineyard on the winery's estate. There's a rugged and meaty quality to the sip, where roasted cranberry, spit-fired meat and cardamom flavors align.

name: Laetitia 2017 Les Galets Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Polished and cohesive aromas of black cherry, cardamom, cappuccino cream and wet, crushed slate show on the dynamic nose of this bottling. The palate explodes in tones of dark fruit, wet clay and crushed gravel, with tannins that softly coat the mouth in a firm veil.

name: La Honda Winery 2017 Sequence Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Light and bright aromas of pomegranate meet with cinnamon and hibiscus on the pinpoint nose of this elegant bottling. The palate is fresh and snappy with raspberry, sumac, ginger, rose petals and more cinnamon spice.

name: La Crema 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Pomegranate, raspberry, carnations, rose petals and toasted sage fall into harmony on the nose of this appellation blend. Black-raspberry and light thyme flavors show on the palate, which deftly balances weight and acid.

name: La Crema 2017 Nine Barrel Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: The winery's special barrel selection, this is a robust, full-bodied and intensely concentrated wine, grippy, layered and ultimately bright. Raspberry and strawberry flavors wrap around silky elegant tannins and integrated oak, showing a hint of clove and cardamom on the finish.

name: La Crema 2017 Fog Veil Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Tangy in strawberry and dark cherry, this impressively full-bodied, richly constructed wine is expansive and elegant. Tremendous structure upholds its core of tannin and oak, finishing with a tangy, high-acid crispness and length.

name: La Crema 2017 Pinot Noir (Los Carneros)
review: This thick, concentrated red is velvety smooth and creamy on the palate, made in an opulent style that displays earthy stemmy wild berry fruit and black tea.

name: La Crema 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tart cherry-pie aromas are quite prominent on the nose of this bottling, yet get leveled by turned soil and wet rocks. The palate is all about tart cherry as well, with more flavors still hidden in the young structure.

name: La Crema 2017 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This full-bodied wine offers seductive, pretty fruit aromas and flavors that reflect ripe black cherries, blueberries and strawberries. It tastes pure and uncomplicated, nicely polished in texture and with a lingering berrylike finish.

name: La Crema 2017 Sealift Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Inviting red fruit and a sharpness of orange peel open the way to a juicy and supple midpalate of robust richness and weight that works well within the integrated tannin and oak. With underlying acidity, it remains fresh on the palate within its hearty fruit and layered texture.

name: La Crema 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This robust wine has a sweet nose of red cherry, cola and vanilla oak, the texture thick and viscously opulent. Baking spice highlights the finish.

name: La Crema 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Stemmy and earthy, this wine is thick in tannin and demure in red fruit, with a simplicity that works well within its medium-bodied context and structure.

name: Kuchan 2017 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Very fresh aromas of raspberry sorbet and hibiscus meet with light brushes of potpourri and wild sage on the nose of this bottling. Dried strawberry and fresh raspberry converge with wild chaparral herbs and earthy mushroom-umami flavors on the palate, where the structure is firm but forgiving.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Giusti Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a longtime vineyard source, this vintage shines in lean, rustic and earthy tones of rhubarb, tangerine and black tea, the texture grainy and full of grip. Round and relatively rich, it has aspects of earthy forest and baking spice within its core, finishing soft and smooth.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Thorn Ridge Ranch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is made from a small site entirely dry-farmed within the Sebastopol Hills. Fresh acidity pierces through spicy tea and clove seasoned around a light, bright and exotic core of orange peel, rhubarb and brambly blackberry and cherry. The finish is lengthy and indelibly memorable.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: A delightfully thoughtful and richly rewarding appellation wine, sourced from Bootlegger's Hill, Treehouse, Zio Tony and other stellar sites, this is hearty in red and blue fruit, cardamom and a taste of cola. Velvety rich and layered, it has well-integrated oak and broad appeal, with a lasting thread of acidity.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Sourced from a slew of outstanding vineyards, including Kanzler and Gap's Crown, this coastal wine is smooth, seductive and earthy, with focused acidity and tension. Orange, blackberry and strawberry are seasoned in cardamom and dried herb.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Gap's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Soft blue fruit, orange tea and rhubarb make for an exotic, tangy and beguiling experience on the palate of this explosive, focused and freshly crisp wine from the famous site. Soft and seductive in texture, it has complexity around rich layers of balanced spice and earthiness.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Keefer Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a site that is dry-farmed, with deep vines, this Keefer is elusive on the nose at first, opening slowly and with grace. Tart, tangy raspberry and cherry glide across a rich, lushly layered palate fresh and exotic in black tea, forest floor and a touch of lemon.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Kanzler Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From this perennially impressive site is this stunning vineyard-designate, earthy in a wealthy array of cola, clove, nutmeg and orange peel. Soft, succulent and layered, it dazzles in fresh, focused black cherry and tart shocks of pomegranate. Black tea finishes things off within a balanced body of full flavor and structured complexity.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Snappy black raspberry, ripe mulberry and plump melon aromas are lifted by cola, fennel pollen and baking spice on the complex nose of this appellation blend. The palate surrounds a red-fruit core of cranberry and strawberry, which is decorated by pinches of wild thyme, dried sage and earthy minerality.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Observations Mt. Carmel Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: The winery's Observations series focuses on unique blocks, and the history and setting of Mt. Carmel, planted by renegade nuns atop the Sta. Rita Hills, makes for a compelling showcase. This bottling starts with aromas of cranberry, pomegranate paste and sandalwood, leading into an earthy palate that's dense with black raspberry and carnation. The fine-tuned structure is most promising. Drink 2022–2042.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Candied strawberry and raspberry-sorbet aromas are generous yet elegant on the nose, enhanced by dried violet and sandalwood scents. There's a great snap of juicy red-cherry and mulberry fruit on the palate, where an energetic texture cuts through the rich ripeness.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Garys' Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Roasted cherry and tart cranberry aromas are dusted with caramel and vanilla bean on the nose of this excellent bottling, which balances ripeness with precision. There's a fruit-punch kick to the tip of the sip, where dark strawberry, baking-spice, slate and earth flavors converge, wrapped in a texture that grows more firm toward the finish.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Pisoni Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Clean and elegant aromas of black-raspberry sorbet are enhanced by light dashes of cardamom on the intense and dark nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate is immensely complex and pleasurable, offering cranberry, orange-rind, jasmine and carnation flavors.

name: Kosta Browne 2017 Rosella's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Lush aromas of poached strawberry and cherry liqueur make for a hedonistic opening to the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate lands beautifully, offering ripe strawberry, pomegranate and candied cherry flavors, yet it sports a relatively light, acid-washed frame that allows accents of mace, star anise and cardamom to shine through.

name: Kokomo 2017 Gopher Hill Block Peters Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Thick, rich and forested, this is a savory block-designate, full-bodied wine that offers tangy red and black fruit, cola and a sweetness of baking spice.

name: Kenwood 2017 The Barn Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Earthy in compost with fruity elements of pomegranate and strawberry, this robustly ripe wine is driven by its jammy richness and abundant oak.

name: Kenwood 2017 Six Ridges Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: High-toned tart red fruit is overtly ripe and the wine full bodied in style, with a wealth of toasted oak and firm tannin.

name: Kendric 2017 Proprietor Grown Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap-Marin County)
review: A rich coating of spices like clove, cedar and vanilla complements good black-cherry flavors in this medium- to full-bodied wine. It is very flavorful, putting the oak spices front and center.

name: Ken Brown 2017 Rancho La Vina Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Smoked black cherry and roasted pork aromas make for a very delicious and inviting nose on this single-vineyard bottling by regional pioneer Ken Brown. The palate snaps with black-raspberry, mulberry and alpine-strawberry tones, with a hickory smoked barbecue note lingering into the finish.

name: Joseph Phelps 2017 Quarter Moon Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is made from 100% estate-grown grapes, an exotic, feral and coastal-influenced expression of the grape that will linger on the palate and mind for awhile. High-toned and rocky in mineral, it offers a wealth of layered blue fruit, focused and structured in laser-like acidity and hits of Asian spice.

name: Joseph Phelps 2017 Pastorale Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This estate-grown wine is creamy smooth on the palate, the tannins silky and elongated. Wild strawberry, dried herb and cardamom accent a thick, densely rich midpalate of structured power with length and undeniable grip. Ultimately, the acidity wins out, balancing it all.

name: Joseph Jewell 2017 Bucher Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy and smoothly exuberant in purple and blue fruit, this lightly ripe wine is balanced and refreshing, with lasting spice notes of pepper, nutmeg and clove. The oak is subtle and supportive, underscoring its savory side.

name: Joseph Jewell 2017 Emerald Pinot Noir (Humboldt County)
review: Dark cherry and exotic wild berry aromas lead to a lively, deep sensation on the palate and more tasty, concentrated fruit nuances subtly spiced with oak, forest-floor and cinnamon notes. Joseph Jewell is building a great reputation for its Humboldt-grown Pinots.

name: Joseph Jewell 2017 Ryan Vineyard Pinot Noir (Humboldt County)
review: Delicious, concentrated black-cherry and black-plum flavors fill this full-bodied wine, coating the palate with richness. Subtle baking spices like cinnamon and nutmeg play around the edges, and moderate acidity and fine-grained tannins add welcome balance to the texture. Best through 2026.

name: Jinsei 2017 Tobias Glen Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: With an opening note of rose petal, this is a cool-climate styled wine, crisp and focused in texture and tension. Cherry cordial, wild strawberry, forest floor and black tea all figure into the silky, supple mix, finishing with a tease of power.

name: JCB 2017 No 11 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Thick, savory and juicy, this wine bristles in forest floor, robust oak and crunchy red fruit. The supple, integrated tannins support a lushly textured opulence and sweet sprinkling of baking spice.

name: JCB 2017 No 7 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Fruity, dense and lush, this is a full-bodied, ripely textured wine that shows varietal earthiness and baking spice. Dark cherry, cola and lavender are lifted by a bite of acidity on the back palate, finishing in toasted vanilla.

name: Jamie Slone Wines 2017 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Raspberry paste, wild tarragon and cracked peppercorns show on the slightly tangy nose of this bottling. More raspberry meets with cranberry on the palate, where wild herbs and pepper spice add nuance.

name: Jamie Slone Wines 2017 Kessler-Haak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Bing cherry, dark strawberry, star anise and nutmeg aromas make for a wondrously inviting nose on this single-vineyard expression. The palate offers full flavors of baked cherry and sea-salt caramel, making for a deliciously hedonistic take on the grape.

name: J. Cage Cellars 2017 Hallberg Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Spicy, earthy and lengthy in acidity, this wine has grip and an impressive grasp of dark cherry and cola, finding balance within its weight and breadth. The texture is appealingly complex.

name: J. Cage Cellars 2017 Van der Kamp Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)
review: This is a smoothly tannic red from the great site, grippy in texture and robustly wrapped in forest floor and clove. It takes hold of the palate in savory character with a touch of black cherry and tree bark, finishing dry and grainy.

name: J Vineyards & Winery 2017 Eastside Knoll Vineyard Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This robust, well-made wine is thick in concentration, with intense tones of red cherry, strawberry and citrus. The acidity boosts an underlying freshness that contrasts well against the firm tannins and toasted oak.

name: J Vineyards & Winery 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This appellation wine offers rich black fruit and cola alongside spice. Its earthiness contrasts well against pronounced oak and balanced weight.

name: Iron Horse 2017 Corral Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: From a part of the estate recently replanted, this is the first bottling to reflect those new vines—a delicately framed exploration of wild strawberry, blueberry and turned earth. Silky smooth texture gives it an inviting quality on the high-toned palate.

name: Indigené 2017 Pinot Noir (Carmel Valley)
review: Rugged and wild cherry aromas meet with rose petals, wild mint and dried meats on the fascinating nose of this bottling. Light sagebrush and dusty earth flavors play against cranberry and thyme on the compelling palate.

name: Hyde Vineyard 2017 Larry Hyde Vineyard Estate Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Toasted oak and turned earth aromas are swept clean by vibrant red cherry and ripe strawberry flavors in this full-bodied, rather broad-textured wine. Made by the Larry Hyde family from its famous vineyard-designate property, it has mouthcoating richness, moderate tannins and great concentration to bring a lingering finish.

name: Holman Ranch 2017 Three Brothers Pinot Noir (Carmel Valley)
review: Warm aromas of baked red cherry and vanilla meet with wet stone and damp earth on the complex nose of this bottling, whose 20% new French oak treatment does shine through. There's a hickory-smoke touch to the palate, where cherry, strawberry, vanilla and star anise spices make for a delicious sip.

name: Highway 12 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Jammy aromas give way to a plummy, berry-like flavor in this straightforward, medium-bodied and soft-textured wine.

name: Hess Collection 2017 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Rounded aromas of red cherry and mulberry pie meet with star anise and allspice on the nose of this bottling. There is a spicy core to the sip, where gingerbread flavors play against rich cherry and dried strawberry elements.

name: Hawley 2017 Oehlman Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This nicely made wine has inviting aromatics of cola and cinnamon, which are followed by thick, layered jammy black fruit. Notes of forest floor, nutmeg and dried herb combine with ease around integrated tannin and oak.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Pinot Noir (Marin County)
review: Wild in forest floor, with a velvety texture, this coastal wine shows elegant layers of plum, wild strawberry and black tea. Additional savory components of clove and cardamom contribute sizzle to its exotic, ethereal nature.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Sevens Bench Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: A spicy nose leads the way to the appellation's earthy funk and a length of cherry vanilla that balances it well. Powerful and weighty, the wine is juicy in orange and tangerine on the midpalate, with firm, supple tannin support and hearty oak.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Arrendell Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a stellar site planted in 1975 to a heritage clone, this wine is aged in French oak for 16 months. Plum, strawberry and blood orange flavors contribute a palate of balanced richness that is both complex and wide in appeal.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Land's Edge Vineyards Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Crisp tones of raspberry and strawberry give this coastal-driven red a sense of place and tangy freshness. The acidity is spread evenly throughout on the robust palate, while a forest floor tone and dense tannins add complexity and balanced weight.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Hailey's Block Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Defined tones of red fruit and tangerine, alongside bright acidity, give this wine undeniable lift and life. It is sourced from a cool-climate block within the Arrendell Vineyard. Herbal in thyme and celery seed, it delights the midpalate with length and energy, expanding on the earthy theme with lasting freshness and specks of white pepper and cardamom.

name: Hartford Court 2017 MacLean's Block Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy, smooth and velvety, this block selection is full bodied, fleshy and lushly opulent, with soft integrated tannin. The ripeness pushes and offers a warmth of rich cherry cola, baked plum and earthy cinnamon.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Seascape Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a panoramic estate site above Occidental in view of the Pacific Ocean, this wine is peaty and feral, with plenty of fruitiness to spare. Beautifully layered and crisp in orange peel and plum, it is aged in 100% French oak, 37% of it new, allowing for structure and grace with lasting teases of clove, pepper and briny sea.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Fog Dance Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: From a foggy site near Forestville, this is a reductive, brawny wine with plenty of weight and backbone. Earthy and herbal, it offers a density of red and black raspberry, with hints of tangerine, the tannins expansive and velvety smooth.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Far Coast Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Near the outpost of Fort Ross, this is a wild, coastal site, and it provides intense grip and power. Juicy and broad in orange peel, dark cherry and strawberry, it has undeniable earthiness and accents of rock and iron, finishing with a lengthy lushness.

name: Hartford Court 2017 Jennifer's Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This rockstar wine is from a cold site south of Sebastopol fed by the Petaluma Gap. Peaty and piquant in licorice, it is rich and balanced, with elongated tannin. Smooth, velvety texture carries strong flavors of blueberry, black raspberry and a tease of grapefruit.

name: Hanzell 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Valley)
review: Reduced, robust and succulent, this earthy red estate wine is energetic and focused, with delicately structured waves of pomegranate and currant. The savory side of the story is defined in cardamom and clove with bursts of black tea and pepper. This balanced, complex wine deserves more time in bottle; enjoy best from 2027–2032.

name: Handley 2017 RSM Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Enticing black-fruit flavors come with a good sense of restraint in this complex, medium-bodied wine. It is nicely dry, nervy in mouthfeel, and energized by moderate tannins. Best through 2028.

name: Handley 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Concentrated black-fruit, mint and tea flavors and a lightly tannic texture characterize this tempting, well-balanced wine. It has a fine sense of depth in the flavors and an appetizing grip on the palate.

name: Handley 2017 Roderick Ranch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This medium-bodied wine is warm and broad, showing wonderful ripe plum and black-cherry flavors matched with subtle acidity and a rounded texture backed by fine-grained tannins. Cinnamon and allspice join with the concentrated fruit flavors to add complexity.

name: Handley 2017 Helluva Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This fragrant and firmly textured wine from a 10-acre vineyard near Boonville, California, offers complex fruit and dark-spice flavors and very good balance.

name: Halcón 2017 Oppenlander Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: This medium-bodied wine blends good, nervy raspberry and sour cherry aromas with savory, woodsy flavors that are not so fruit-centered but tempting and complex, from black tea to rhubarb and mushroom. Good acidity and moderate tannins form a firm texture that will enable at least mid-term aging. Best through 2025.

name: Goldeneye 2017 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This attractive, ultrasmooth, full-bodied wine gently wraps black cherries and mulled plums in soft tannins and modest acidity for a warm, mouthcoating effect.

name: Goldeneye 2017 Confluence Lower Bench Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This wine is very ripe, generous and full bodied, offering warm strawberry, red- and black-cherry aromas followed by equally fruity flavors and baking-spice accents from new oak barrels. It's seductive and velvety on the palate.

name: Goldeneye 2017 Confluence Hillside Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This full-bodied wine leads with enticing wood-smoke and dark-spice aromas, followed by expansive, attractive and concentrated black-fruit flavors wrapped in firm but velvety tannins. Aged in 60% new French barrels, it shows a certain swagger.

name: Goldeneye 2017 Split Rail Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: A very concentrated core of delicious black fruits gives great focus to this full-bodied, powerful but polished wine made from nine different clones of Pinot Noir. Layers of black cherry, black currant and black tea are energized by lively acidity and moderate tannins, setting the wine up for good age-ability. This is the most impressive Split Rail since at least 2012. Best through 2027.

name: Goldeneye 2017 Confluence Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: A distinctive, roasted, smoky aroma tops this full-bodied and well-structured wine before black-fruit flavors and an array of clove, cinnamon and cedar kick in.

name: Goldeneye 2017 The Narrows Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Rich, spicy and delicious, this full-bodied wine radiates ripe fruit and yummy baking-spice notes from new oak. It feels warm and broad in texture and offers plenty of mouthfilling cherry-pie flavors.

name: Gehricke 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Made in a lush, opulent style, with sizable tannin and vanilla oak, this wine offers bursts of cranberry, currant and strawberry, with a hit of blood orange that lasts through the finish.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Rochioli-Allen Vineyards Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Lifted and endurably fresh, this impressive wine opens in tones of wild strawberry, dried herb and forest floor, with a supple midpalate that teases a hint of power, settling into tension and texture. While light in style, it still has weight and breadth, finishing with balance and grace.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Light in the glass, this bottling is earthy in tones of carnation, hibiscus and cranberry. It's wound up and a bit shy on the palate, where dried cranberry and cracked peppercorn show alongside a hint of tar.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Martaella Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Savory black tea meets crisp cranberry and pomegranate in this bottling from the great site. Showing powerful, persistent tannins and a silky smooth feel, graceful oak tones are well integrated and it finishes on flavors of rose and cardamom.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Hallberg Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Forceful in black tea, warm spice and forest floor, this bold, beautifully layered vineyard-designate wine is complemented by underlying red tones of pomegranate, cranberry and wild strawberry. Balanced, complex and eminently appealing, it features silky, elegant tannins.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Sanford & Benedict Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tightly woven aromas of boysenberry, dark, loamy soil and forest wood come through on the nose of this bottling from a historic vineyard. There's a strong sense of crushed rocks on the palate, where ripe and rich flavors of dark berry, cherry compote and black spice are wrapped in firm tannins. Drink through 2037.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Lancel Creek Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a site near Occidental that's farmed by Valdez & Sons, this wine leaves an indelible impression—a sophisticated and complex take on the grape. Earthy and grippy in tannins, it becomes expansive, exuberant and fresh on the palate, revealing a mix of forest floor, white pepper and pomegranate that skirts the edge of cool, coastal intensity.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Hallberg Vineyard Dijon Clones Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is sourced from three distinct blocks planted to the Dijon clone, which is known to impart earth and spice in great measure. Juicy and elegant, this tastes of black tea, clove, nutmeg and rhubarb, with a broad feel of silky tannin. Structured and vibrant in acidity, it offers a complete package of complex flavor and texture.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Fort Ross Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
review: From a cool, coastal site that stretches from 1,200- to 1,700-feet in elevation, this red is delicate in tones of strawberry, citrus and forest floor. An accent of mint tea rides above the layerd core, all framed by expertly structured tannins and vibrant acidity.

name: Gary Farrell 2017 Russian River Selection Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is an outstanding showcase of the appellation, sourced from such stellar sites as Hallberg, Rochioli and Martaella. Fragrant, fruity and herbal, with an exotically spicy undercurrent, it shows a length of fresh pomegranate, raspberry and white pepper all laced by crisp acidity.

name: Gallo Signature Series 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Primarily from the Gallo family's Olson Ranch, with 8% of the fruit sourced from Sleepy Hollow Vineyard, this voluptuous expression starts with inviting aromas of red cherry and vanilla that are cut by slate, soil and forest herbs. The hedonistic palate is oak-driven, offering vanilla, caramel and smoke flavors that wrap around the black cherry and cola core.

name: Fritz Underground 2017 Reserve Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Based around tangy flavors of cranberry, orange and pomegranate, this wine is floral and earthy, a mix of forest and clove that accentuate the oak. The finish is smooth and approachable.

name: Frei Brothers 2017 Sonoma Reserve Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Silky smooth in feel and showing a great depth of fruit, this red is richly layered and reminiscent in all the best ways of cherry pie. It's full in feel and cradled in toasted oak.

name: Freeman 2017 Akiko's Cuvée Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Generous, fruity and gentle, this full-flavored wine boasts dark cherries, blueberries, accents of baking spices and a very smooth, rich texture that seems to melt on the palate. It has ripeness verging on sweetness, and is easy to sip and enjoy.

name: Freeman 2017 Yu-Ki Estate Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Pretty, layered and polished to a T, this elegant wine is a clear standout. Ripe black cherries, hints of baking spices and forest floor fill the aroma while plush, generous cherry and plum notes coat the palate. Soft tannins and a creamy texture complete the picture.

name: Freeman 2017 Gloria Estate Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Excellent depth and complexity bring this concentrated wine to a high level of deliciousness. It offers deep, brooding aromas of black cherry, clove and forest floor followed by mouthfilling and focused flavors of black cherry and sour cherry that linger on the finish.

name: Frank Family 2017 Lewis Vineyard Reserve Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This is brawny and somewhat reduced reserve-level wine, with a generous presence of oak and tannin. Textured black fruit is jammy and concentrated in nature, with a richness that persists through a lengthy finish.

name: Frank Family 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This is generously ripe and substantial in approach, offering rich, dense flavors of black cherry, cranberry and cinnamon cola. A touch of forest floor adds to the earthy mix, as a light texture guides the palate to an oaky finish.

name: Foursight 2017 Paraboll Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Full bodied but spare and savory-spicy in flavor, this wine shows rustic, tangy and earthy elements that keep it interesting and complex. A light-garnet color leads to cherry, cinnamon and black-tea flavors.

name: Foursight 2017 Charles Vineyard Clone 5 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Flavors that are light and earthy-savory give this medium-bodied, unfined and unfiltered wine an austere feel. If offers underripe fruit notes, spicy tea accents and a brownish tinge in the color.

name: Foursight 2017 Charles Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Medium bodied and restrained in flavor, this lean, native-yeast wine offers savory mushroom and tart cherry on the palate, and a tangy mouthfeel.

name: Foursight 2017 Charles Vineyard Zerø Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This tangy wine fermented with wild yeast first offers tart cranberry and tea flavors, light to medium body and then subtle baking-spice notes that slowly develop on the palate and linger on the finish.

name: Four Vines 2017 The Maverick Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: It's hard to find a worthy Pinot Noir for less than $25, but here's a winner. Hearty aromas of dark cherry and gingerbread lead into a spicy palate of bay leaf, ripe strawberry and fresh blackberry jam. A chalky texture rises into the finish.

name: Fort Ross Vineyard 2017 Fort Ross Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
review: Robustly built and yet elegant throughout, this estate-grown wine is forested with black-tea notes that meld against a foundation of bright red berry. Ample tannin structure evolves in the glass to show a nice supple quality and flecks of crushed rock and mineral.

name: Fort Ross Vineyard 2017 Top of Land Fort Ross Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
designation: Top of Land Fort Ross Vineyard
review: From the highest-elevation blocks of the estate vineyard, this red is cool-climate personified, with a reductive touch on the nose. Toasted oak wraps around meaty waves of cherry, pomegranate and forest floor, the tannins intense yet supple and silky, the wine finishing in a tease of lavender.

name: Fort Ross Vineyard 2017 The Terraces Fort Ross Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
review: This is from an estate site close to the ocean and planted entirely to the Calera clone within one terraced block. High toned in currant, forest and tea, it is earthy throughout, with bright, underlying freshness and crisp layers of black cherry, raspberry and orange.

name: Fort Ross Vineyard 2017 Stagecoach Road Fort Ross Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
review: From a selection of Calera grapes grown on the extreme coast, this wine offers spice, wax and edgy acidity. Pomegranate, tangerine, cherry and mint flavors follow along a structured, complex and lengthy palate of balanced tannin and oak, finishing in lasting earthiness.

name: Foley 2017 Rancho Santa Rosa Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Strong, earthy tones of wild sagebrush and cola spice decorate the black cherry and plum aromas on the nose of this bottling. Baked cherry and raspberry flavors pop on the palate, where cardamom spice and earthy herbs add nuance.

name: Foley 2017 T Anchor Ranch Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: A light rosewater aroma leads to a ripe pomegranate expression on the nose of this bottling. There's an elegant array of violet, lavender and lilac on the sip, as well as black plum and cardamom, which lead into a slightly bitter finish.

name: Foley 2017 JA Ranch Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Focused aromas of slate, pomegranate and rose command the nose of this bottling. Light cranberry and thyme flavors come through on the mellow palate, along with a dried purple-flower accent.

name: Flowers 2017 Sea View Ridge Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is a feral, earthy and layered wine from the famous coastal site, woven with compost, black tea and savory cardamom character. The fruit is tart and intriguing, a mix of pomegranate, rhubarb and wild strawberry that match well with the accents and grippy texture.

name: Flowers 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This complex wine is thick in tannin and structure, with nuanced underlying elegance. Textured, it offers dried herb, clove, wild strawberry and a twist of blood orange on the palate, with acidity interwoven within the integrated oak.

name: Figge 2017 Dos Amigos Theresa Noelle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dark cherry, dried cranberry, potpourri and a saline element show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is quite ripe with baked cherry, roasted plum, clove and root-beer spices, proving a bit rich.

name: FEL 2017 Ferrington Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Luscious, delicious fruit and spice notes coat the palate and linger nicely on the finish of this concentrated, layered and luxurious wine. It's full in feel, integrating rich tannins with layers of ripe fruit and subtle baking spice, making it tempting and delicious.

name: FEL 2017 Wendling Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: The firm structure of this wine frames great depth of flavor and a fascinating core of black currants and black cherries for a mouthfiling and rich but also vibrant expression. It is full bodied, well balanced and complex. Best through 2025.

name: FEL 2017 Savoy Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Precise and very focused, this medium- to full-bodied wine offers vibrant red- and black-fruit flavors, great acidity and moderate tannins. Delicate oak spices trim the delicious, energized fruit flavors and everything tastes balanced and well defined. Best through 2025.

name: Fallon Place 2017 Estines Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: High-toned and softly integrated in tannin and oak, this wine has exotic spice notes of cardamom, nutmeg and pepper that wrap around light elements of cherry and pomegranate.

name: Etude 2017 Forte Fiddlestix Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dark-berry aromas meet with a touch of warm toast on the nose of this rich single-vineyard expression. It's big on the palate, with loads of flavors and strong acidity, showing boysenberry, red currant and white pepper. A bit more time is required for everything to align.

name: EnRoute 2017 Northern Spy Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Delicate notes of cranberry, rhubarb and orange peel are met by a thick, grainy texture and powerful warm spice in this rich, full-bodied wine. Baking spice, cedar and candied cherry round it out on the lengthy, concentrated finish.

name: EnRoute 2017 Marty's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earth aromas of soil and black tea sport the nose of this wine that's sourced from a cool spot in the Green Valley. Black cherry, plum and cardamom revolve around supple, lengthy tannins and integrated oak.

name: EnRoute 2017 Bucher Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From the spectacular site along Westside Road, this red is smooth, herbal and earthy, with edges of rhubarb and orange. Integrated, complex and transfixing, it showcases bright red cherry and strawberry against warm spice and a hint of white pepper, finishing complete and focused on a burst of acidity.

name: EnRoute 2017 Amber Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Licorice, black raspberry and blueberry highlight this densely packed wine. With notes of black olive and licorice, it retains a sparkling array of baking spice and cola flavors through the lengthy finish.

name: EnRoute 2017 Les Pommiers Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine opens in pretty aromas of rose petal and lavender, inviting one in with additional tones of dark cherry, raspberry and black tea. Textured and robustly layered, it shows components of dried herb, turned earth and clove on the palate, finishing on supple tannins and mild oak.

name: Emeritus 2017 Pinot Hill West Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Estate-grown, this is from a western-facing slope of the property in the Sebastopol Hills. Turned earth, sage and a warming depth of cardamom coat the plush, balanced palate, developing elegant layers of black cherry and licorice. The finish is a mix of dark chocolate and vanilla bean, with acidity always at the forefront.

name: Emeritus 2017 Hallberg Ranch Wesley's Reserve Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a juicy, effusive wine, made from several of the vineyard's prized blocks year after year. Pomegranate, rhubarb and dried herb highlight a length of concentrated blackberry and rich robust tannin. Elegant from start to end, the finish is a mix of black pepper and black tea, and is earthy in forest floor.

name: Emeritus 2017 Pinot Hill Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a dry-farmed Sebastopol Hills site substantially cooler than the producer's marquee Hallberg Ranch, this is sublime, nuanced and delicate wine, tightly wound, with grip and crispness. Cherry, strawberry and raspberry come into sharp focus around sea spray and minty forest, the finish ethereally minded.

name: Emeritus 2017 Hallberg Ranch Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: With Winemaker David Lattin now at the helm, this vintage packs a punch of grace and power. Rose, lavender and earth tones accent a juicy black-cherry core, with crisp acidity sitting alongside a hint of nutmeg.

name: Educated Guess 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Juicy and dark, this red wine is full bodied in style and ripeness, with soft simple layers of tart red fruit. Dried herb, cola and black tea add to the flavor.

name: E16 2017 Doctor's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Baked raspberry and cherry make for a very plush nose on this bottling, which also benefits from brown spice and a hint of roasted meat on the nose. The palate is simultaneously plush and tense, combining ripe wild strawberry flavors with a spicy backdrop of star anise, mace and dried ginger, popping with a Sichuan peppercorn sensation on the finish.

name: E16 2017 Firefall Pinot Noir (California)
review: This charming, floral and fruity wine has a lot going for it, from subtle violet and cherry aromas to a smooth mouthfeel and nicely articulated cherry-candy flavors.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Deviate Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This deviates from the producer's more typical single-vineyard designates, combining grapes from two sites: Jentoft and Putnam. It opens in rose petal and black licorice, finding textured layers of juicy black cherry, black tea and tobacco that integrate with firm robust tannin weight and structure. It finishes with enduring intensity.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 McDougall Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
review: From a site 1,100-feet up on a ridge overlooking the Pacific Ocean, this wine offers rich cherry and strawberry tones alongside dried herbs. A balanced framework of lifted acidity propels it along.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Fox Den Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Tones of grapefruit, strawberry and red cherry kick off this immensely textured, expressive wine from a site along Stoetz Ridge above Occidental. Warm spice dots the landscape of lively freshness and supple tannins, with depths of flavor and balanced richness.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Dutton Ranch Emerald Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Tangy and compelling, this is a complex, intensely earthy wine, with wisps of dried herb and fresh sea air. The palate offers a mix of orange, blueberry and raspberry, with persistent acidity. Baking spice, dried herb and forest floor accents meld with the rounded, supple tannins.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Redwood Ridge Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a 32-acre site above Annapolis, this is a gorgeously memorable wine that is dark and brooding in the glass. It unveils slowly to show rose, stem, lavender and sage as well as crisp red fruit. Lifted yet structured in feel, it shows power and intensity in perfect tandem with grace and balance.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Van Der Kamp Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)
review: Stemmy and earthy, this is a wildly exotic red wine, tense in sage and clove. Mountain strawberry, black cherry and rhubarb meet a wall of black tea, with compelling streaks of white pepper and firm tannin, combining for a forceful, balanced finish.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Freestone Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Tasting of plummy Craisins, black tea and pomegranate, this wine hails from a cool site influenced by coastal winds, and it shows high-toned acidity and tension across the light-bodied, balanced palate. Complex and spicy in tobacco and clove, it finishes supple and structured.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Angel Camp Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Ample oak spices meet bright and ripe fruit flavors in this full-bodied well-balanced wine. Notes of cinnamon, cedar and clove lead to red and black cherries wrapped in good, firm acidity and tannins. It's an appetizing and palate-cleansing pour.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Devil's Gulch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Marin County)
review: This wine pits superfocused fruit against a taut frame of fine-grained tannins and nervy acidity for excellent balance. Aromas of fresh black cherry, black tea and forest floor lead to lively rhubarb and sour cherry backed by richer nuances that emerge on the finish. Best through 2027.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Docker Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Concentrated fruit flavors are wrapped in a firm, well-polished texture of fine-grained tannins and fresh acidity in this wine. Bright acidity propels vibrant flavors of black cherry and black tea across the palate and through the finish. It is quietly powerful and well structured for mid-term aging. Best through 2025.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2017 Azaya Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: This wine is an eye-opener, offering dazzling fruit flavors and a vibrant texture that wakes up the palate. Concentrated cherry and strawberry flavors follow aromas of freshly crushed berries, rosemary and baking spices for a broad spectrum of complexity. Best through 2027.

name: Dunstan 2017 Durell Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Savory in tea and forest, this wine offers bold, bombastic tannin weight and intensity alongside subdued dark fruit.

name: DuMOL 2017 Wester Reach Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Made from four stellar sites within the appellation, this stunning wine shows rich red and blue fruit. Spice and forest floor tones intertwine alongside firm, supple tannins and balanced acidity, finishing with impressive length and intensity.

name: DRNK 2017 Hallberg Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Light, bright and effusively floral, this wine is both earthy and stemmy, with fresh layers of wild strawberry and raspberry. Notes of cardamon and violet filter through on the nose and palate, giving the wine room to breathe and develop further beauty in the glass.

name: DRNK 2017 Cuvée Cavers Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Light, floral and stony in minerality, this well-made wine impresses in its mix of orange, rhubarb and earthiness. Exotic spice rides along the bite of tension that exists via fresh, focused acidity and supple tannin structure.

name: Drew 2017 Estate Mid-Slope Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)
review: Wonderfully focused black-fruit flavors and a warm wrap of fine-grained tannins make this wine irresistible. It's generous, expansive and deep, yet superbly balanced and elegant in texture, too. Winemaker Jason Drew used 25% whole clusters in the fermentation and aged the wine in 30% new French oak.

name: Drew 2017 Valenti Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)
review: Wonderful baking-spice aromas combine with ripe and vibrant black-cherry and cranberry flavors in this energized, medium-bodied wine. A great structure of moderate tannins and lively acidity make the mouthfeel extra fresh and bode well for midterm aging. Made by Jason Drew from a micro vineyard on a steep slope overlooking Anderson Valley. Best through 2026.

name: Drew 2017 Wendling Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This wine is very tight in structure, high in acid and tannins, clamping down its black-cherry and rhubarb flavors. Not as ripe and generous as the other Drew Pinots in 2017, it holds back on the fruit and has a steely texture.

name: Drew 2017 Morning Dew Ranch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Wood smoke and clove aromas lead into broad, vivid black-cherry and baking-spice flavors in this full bodied and well balanced wine. Made famous by Williams Selyem, this hillside vineyard gives good fruit acidity and moderate tannins that liven up the mouthfeel. Best through 2026.

name: Drew 2017 Joshua's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This aromatic and well-structured wine is concentrated and delicious, and a little tight with tannins and acidity at this stage. Made with 40% whole clusters in the fermentation, it offers tempting red-cherry, cranberry and damp earth nuances, lightly shaded by baking spices. Best from 2023.

name: Drew 2017 Mid-Elevation Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)
review: Great warmth and concentration make this full-bodied wine especially appealing. It fills the aromas with cinnamon, vanilla and red cherries, and follows with ripe but bright black cherry, black tea and rhubarb flavors that don't quit, but seem to swell and grow with each sip. Best through 2026.

name: Drew 2017 The Fog-Eater Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This lively, beautifully balanced wine pairs delicious fresh cherry and persimmon flavors with vibrant acidity and moderate tannins for a rather taut mouthfeel that signals excellent aging potential. Best through 2026.

name: Drew 2017 Estate Field Selections Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)
review: This wine is nervy, complex and fascinating, blending bright red cherry, riper black cherry and nuances of black tea and forest floor on a sleek structure of lifted acidity and moderate tannins. Best through 2028.

name: Dragonette 2017 Black Label Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: There's an intriguing earthy core to the nose of this bottling, with aromas of grilled pork, muddled mulberry and cranberries roasted over an oak fire. Similar fruit flavors show on the palate, along with brighter flashes of red currant, a tang of orange rind, a brush of iodine and a smoky show of mesquite.

name: Dot Wines 2017 Lolita Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: A straightforward, moderately structured approach makes for a wine marked by toasted oak, a touch of vanilla and dark, dense black cherry. With a light grip of tannin, it provides a nice contrast of fruit and savory character.

name: Donum 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: A wealth of juicy black fruit, forest floor and tea comes to the fore of this bold, ripe wine, thickly concentrated and solidly built with structure. The tannins grip the palate and emphasize the overall intensity, as toasted oak and flecks of cola accent the lengthy finish.

name: Donum 2017 Heritage Clones Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Smoky cherry and a wisp of crisp blood orange add brightness to this tightly structured, hearty and earthy red, which is full bodied and densely concentrated in its youth. A dustiness convenes on the texture, adding complex hints of crushed rock and black tea.

name: Donum 2017 Reserve Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Deeply fragrant in rose petal, this reserve-worthy wine is inviting all around, with a backbone of savory earth, char, clove and forest floor. Structured and complex, it has layers of grippy texture and integrated oak, finishing in feral notes of cardamom and black tea.

name: Donum 2017 White Barn Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Warm baking spice and clove season an elegant core of refined tannin and silky blackberry and cherry in this lighter style wine. A grip of tannin adds weight and complexity as toasty oak integrates with the fruit.

name: Donum 2017 Year of the Rooster Mikado Tree Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
designation: Year of the Rooster Mikado Tree Single Vineyard
review: Blueberry, blackberry and spicy cardamom dominate in this bold and brawny wine. Bright acidity underscores its delicious core of berry and spice flavors. The tannins and oak are substantial yet fully supportive to the whole.

name: Donum 2017 Year of the Rooster West Slope Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
designation: Year of the Rooster West Slope Single Vineyard
review: Bright acidity and cardamom thread throughout the concentrated core of this small-production wine, the structure and substance undeniably generous. Candied strawberry, black cherry and pomegranate give a rich consistency to the flavor profile, weaving in the tannins and oak with ease.

name: Donum 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Thick and viscous in brawny, concentrated flavors of baked strawberry, savory earth and an intensity of caramel, this is a well-structured, full-bodied and robustly ripe wine from the winery estate. Black tea, compost and rhubarb furl around its edges.

name: Donum 2017 Year of the Rooster Angel Camp Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
designation: Year of the Rooster Angel Camp
review: Rich, ripe and warmly spiced, this full-bodied wine is also firmly structured, with fine-grained tannins and subtle acidity. Luxurious aromas of cinnamon, clove and black cherry precede opulent fruit and oolong tea flavors. Best through 2025. Best through 2025.

name: Donum 2017 Year of the Rooster Single Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
designation: Year of the Rooster Single Vineyard
review: This beautifully balanced and concentrated wine has all the makings of a classic. Its firm but seamless structure supports beautiful black fruits and dark spices that play off each other while vibrant acidity lifts the mouthfeel through a lingering finish. Best through 2028.

name: Donnachadh 2017 Hillside Heritage Vines Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Aromas of bay leaf, mint tea, pine needle, iron and sour cherry show on the layered nose of this bottling from Winemaker Ernst Storm. There's a fascinating convergence of those green-herb elements and ripe flashes of boysenberry and black raspberry on the palate, with hints of tobacco and tarragon throughout.

name: Domaine Eden 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: This is a great example of the wines being made by Mount Eden and other classic producers in this region. Aromas of ripe raspberry, crushed stone and dried herbs lead into a taut palate where cranberry skin, wild sage and tobacco flavors ride vibrant acidity.

name: Domaine de la Côte 2017 Bloom's Field Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: For 12.5% alcohol, this is a very brawny and hearty bottling, starting with aromas of wild berry, smoke and pine needle. Leathery tannins frame the sip, where wild sage and eucalyptus flavors play against rich elderberry and forest berries.

name: Domaine Anderson 2017 Walraven Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Delicious, ripe fruit flavors form the core of this generous but well-balanced wine that almost bursts with raspberry, black cherry and mint character. It's technically light bodied, but very mouthfilling. Best to drink through 2025.

name: Domaine Anderson 2017 Pinoli Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Strong coffee and earth aromas top this vibrant and full-bodied wine, offering a different take on the varietal while backing them up with solid, concentrated black cherry and sour cherry on the palate. Moderate tannins and good acidity bode well for aging through 2026.

name: Domaine Anderson 2017 Dach Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Distinctive earthy, leafy, tea-like aromas and rich black-cherry flavors give this medium-bodied, tiny-production wine plenty to appreciate. It is well balanced, concentrated and a bit tannic, which adds a nice bit of grip to the texture. Best through 2027.

name: Domaine Anderson 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This full-bodied wine is deep and flavorful, topping black-fruit flavors with layers of complex black-tea and forest-floor accents. The mouthfeel is broad but grippy, showing moderate tannins and good acidity. Best through 2027.

name: Director's Cut 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Simple, thick in tannin and oak, this hearty red has a density to the baked fruit at its core, which is lightly spiced in cola and cinnamon.

name: Decroux 2017 La Encantada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tight aromas of black plum, rose petal, lavender and candied sour cherry kick off the nose of this bottling. The palate offers mulberry and exotic spices, carried across a very light texture and delicately thin mouthfeel.

name: Decroux 2017 John Sebastiano Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Red currant meets with extreme floral tones of potpourri, dried lavender and patchouli on the nose of this bottling. There is a rhubarb kick to the sip, which also brings carnations and rose petals to this floral arrangement.

name: De Loach 2017 Marin Pinot Noir (Marin County)
review: This distinctive and complex wine offers a bouquet of dark fruit, bay leaf and black tea that are joined in the flavors by black cherry and clove. Excellent depth of flavor, full body and layering add to its quality and interest. Best through 2024.

name: De Loach 2017 Maboroshi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a site in the Sebastopol Hills, a relatively cool-climate neighborhood of the appellation, this wine dazzles in a contrast of sweet and sour red fruit, retaining high-toned acidity and freshness beneath powerful ripeness.

name: De Loach 2017 Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Savory and thick in brushy texture, this wine offers crunchy red fruit and complex notions of forest floor and dried herb. Balanced and light in ripeness, it delivers delicate flavor and composure.

name: De Loach 2017 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a thick, reductive and powerfully built wine, duly ripe in dark cherry, black licorice and lasting in earthy intensity. It delivers bold, decisive flavors buoyed by strong oak and firm tannin.

name: De Loach 2017 La Bienvenue Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From estate-grown grapes, this small-production red delivers thick, brawny and succulent layers of bright acidity and big-shouldered red and black cherry. Oak and tannin play prominently on the palate, adding texture and weighty complexity.

name: De Loach 2017 Les Parcelles Cachées Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This big, flavorful wine was made in small amounts, delivering a thick, ripe and robust experience on the palate. Forest floor, dried herb and caramelized oak add to its savory nature and intensity.

name: Davis Bynum 2017 Dutton Ranches Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From the Dutton Lorenzo Vineyard near Forestville, and the inaugural vintage at that, this impresses in earthy funk and rich, lively red and black cherry. Smooth, supple tannins support a full-bodied ripeness and weight that will appeal to fans of a bigger style of Pinot.

name: Davis Bynum 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Thick, tannic and generous in oak, this is hearty, full-bodied red from a mix of vineyards in the appellation. Bright threads of orange, strawberry and dried herb pepper the core, which leads to a luxuriously opulent finish.

name: Davies 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a selection of vineyards, including the single-designates Nobles and Piedra Libra, this is a satisfying appellation wine, tangy and tart in high-toned acidity and crisp red fruit. Layered and textured, it has a touch of forest floor and dried herb alongside firm, supple tannin.

name: Davies 2017 Piedra Libre Vineyards Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Earthy and spicy, this wine is intensely flavored and textured, offering a juicy midpalate of pomegranate and crisp orange. From a site only eight miles from the ocean, it shows a thickness of tannin and minerality, with hints of forest floor and stone.

name: Davies 2017 Nobles Vineyard Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
review: Boysenberry, leather, turned earth and forest rise to the fore of this coastal wine, from a high-elevation site above the ocean. Juicy and offering length and breadth, it is both rich and riddled in acid-driven tension, providing powerful flavor and intensity.

name: Davies 2017 Three Amigos Vineyard Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: This wine has a touch of reduction and robust oak on the nose, coming from a site near the Napa Marina. The cool climate contributes firm, robust tannin weight and robust red fruit.

name: Darioush 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Made in a robust, powerfully concentrated style, this wine is earthy and stemmy, with a carbonic liveliness beneath the weight. Thick, rich and creamily lush on the palate, it is well made and richly layered.

name: Darcie Kent Vineyards 2017 Noir Pinot Noir (California)
review: Potpourri, black plum and gingerbread aromas show on the nose of this bottling from multiple counties. There is decent acidity to the palate, where dried berry flavors are lifted by clove, star anise and cola spice.

name: Daniel 2017 Grand Vent Vineyard Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: With only 45 cases made, this wine impresses in its grasp of the cool-climate site and appellation—the vineyard once known as Clary Ranch. Smoky oak and stemmy dried herb make for an undeniable earthiness that complements the high-strung acidity and crisp flavors of strawberry and rhubarb.

name: D & L Carinalli 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy fruit presents as thick and concentrated on the palate in this estate wine, made in small amounts. Textured and grippy, it has highlights of caramel, chocolate and jammy strawberry.

name: Cuvaison 2017 Swan Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Los Carneros)
review: This is a micro-lot of wine meant to showcase one block of the producer's estate planted to the Swan clone, an heirloom clone of the variety. Herbal and tangy in cranberry and rhubarb, it shows subtle, integrated oak and tight, focused acidity, offering structure within light concentration.

name: Crux 2017 ACE Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Two barrels of this wine were made to benefit the Anova Center for Education (ACE), a school close to the heart of the producers. With grapes from the Cartograph Estate in Sebastopol Hills, it shines in earthy cardamom, black tea and stemmy intensity, the berries partially carbonic fermented. Plum, dark cherry and strawberry round out a lasting richness on the palate. Only sold in magnum.

name: Croix 2017 Old Manzana Slope Manzana Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine has a lively nose of peppery baking spice, followed by rich layers of black cherry, black tea and high-toned acidity. Lasting in velvety texture, it shows an underlying element of crushed rock and dusty grip.

name: Croix 2017 Thomas Creek Thomas Road Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Lush and plush in velvety rich flavors of brown sugar, baked cherry and strawberry, this is a robust and generously red wine, finished in vanilla and baking spice.

name: Croix 2017 South Block Six Platt Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From the coastal site overlooking the ocean, this is a thick, viscous red structured and lushly layered in sweet cola, plum, bacon and ash. A finishing touch of tobacco and turned earth accent its full-bodied intensity.

name: Croix 2017 Narrow Gauge Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a mélange of vineyards, including Manzana, Calesa and Thomas Road, this red is earthy in black tea and baking spice, with focused acidity and a stemmy freshness that presents as both wild and incredibly appealing. Crisp rhubarb, red cherry and pomegranate are bold, burly and richly layered.

name: Cornerstone 2017 Fiddlestix Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Seared cherry, scorched earth and singed sagebrush aromas meet with prominent oak on the nose of this bottling. The oak is quite strong on the sip as well, where ripe cherries and seared wood flavors dominate.

name: Cordant 2017 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Dark hibiscus, raspberry compote, ripe plum and a touch of oak aromas show on the nose of this appellation blend. The palate is full of texture though slightly reserved on flavors, combining red plum and wood smoke with tangy acidity and a light dusting of herbs.

name: Cordant 2017 Kessler-Haak Vineyards Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Concentrated aromas of blackberry, cardamom and vanilla cream and prominent oak show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Winemaker Tyler Russell definitely explores the riper, richer side of this grape, with thick flavors of roasted cherry and caramel on the palate, yet with enough acid and grip to provide balance.

name: Cordant 2017 Radian Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Bing and black-cherry aromas meet with dark hibiscus and minty earth on the rich nose of this bottling from a celebrated vineyard on the southwest corner of the appellation. The palate lands with power and smoke, blending blackberry, oak and prominent structure into a finish that lingers with vanilla and French toast spices.

name: Cordant 2017 Escolle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Red plum, dried flowers, hints of baking spice and a touch of oak show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is framed by a searing acidity, blending layers of richer caramel with pops of snappy raspberry fruit.

name: Copain Wines 2017 Tous Ensemble Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Made in a light, buzzy style, this red is juicy in wild strawberry, accompanied by savory threads of black tea and forest floor. Soft integrated tannins make it appealing in the glass, the acidity lively and complementary.

name: Copain Wines 2017 Côte Bannie Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Made in a light, delicate style, with ample acidity and a minimum of subtle French oak, this tastes of cinnamon and cherry, with citrus-laden undertones that emphasize its brightness.

name: Copain Wines 2017 Les Voisins Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This elegant, superbly balanced wine offers tempting, just-ripe fruit flavors on a medium to full body, and has a lively, slightly grippy mouthfeel that showcases the vivid red- and black-cherry flavors and subtle baking-spice accents. Best through 2027.

name: Copain Wines 2017 Edmeades Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This flavorful and well-balanced wine offers generous strawberry and cherry flavors, a broad mouthfeel and just a touch of tannin for texture. It's full bodied, moderately spicy and quite expressive overall. Best through 2027.

name: Copain Wines 2017 Abel Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Opulent and spicy, this medium-bodied wine shows tempting dark spices of clove and toasted oak along with ripe black-cherry flavors and a velvety texture. A good structure of fine-grained tannins adds an appetizing grip. Best through 2027.

name: Copain Wines 2017 Maggy Hawk Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This relatively light-bodied wine is broad and delicious, layering baking spices like cinnamon, vanilla and clove over considerable black-cherry flavors. It highlights the Anderson Valley's fresh fruitiness and supple texture.

name: Cooper-Garrod 2017 Two Vineyards Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Aromas of stewed and roasted cherries stuffed with bay leaf, eucalyptus and sandalwood show on the nose of this bottling. There's a rocky and rusty minerality to the sip, where dried cherry flavors meet with thyme, dried sage and clove.

name: Cooper Wells 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Lighter in the glass, this bottling begins with aromas of raspberry and roasted cherry tomato. There's a pleasantly zesty character to the sip, where tangy red-plum flavors enliven the tongue and lead into a toasted nut finish.

name: Colluvial 2017 Las Nenas Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Earthy and gamy aromas kick off the nose of this bottling, which quickly veers toward dried cranberry. There is a solid sizzle to the sip, where wild plum and mulberry flavors pair well with touches of tarragon and anise.

name: Cobden Wini 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Fleshy and fruity, with a depth of black tea and dried herb, this light, bright and delicate wine shows good integration of both tannin and oak. The fruit is a mix of blood orange and wild strawberry, flecked in cardamom and white pepper.

name: Cobb 2017 Wendling Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Beautifully focused black-fruit and subtle dark spice aromas give way to rich but not heavy black cherry, black tea and forest floor on the palate in this dazzling medium- to full-bodied wine. Light tannins and good acidity set it up for drinking now through 2027.

name: Cobb 2017 Doc's Ranch Pommard and 114 Selection Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a site that neighbors Coastlands, in the foggy moors above Occidental, this wine is unabashedly earthy and ethereal. Tasting lightly of red fruit, orange peel and tea, it has a lingering persistence of sea air, almost as if fog had been captured in the bottle.

name: Cobb 2017 Coastlands Vineyard Old Firs Block Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Made from older vines within the famous estate site, this resplendent wine offers fresh strawberry, blueberry and perfumed rose—bursts that are underscored by linear, focused acidity. Tasting of the woods, with a crunch to the texture, it finishes in a refreshing tease of orange peel.

name: Cobb 2017 Emmaline Ann Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Textured and earthy, with a firmness of tannin, this youthful wine is high toned and full of energy. Layers of crunchy pomegranate, rhubarb and black tea mingle and meld across the palate, seasoned in cardamom and white pepper.

name: Cobb 2017 Diane Cobb Coastlands Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Named to honor one of the vineyard's visionary founders, Diane Cobb comes from a 1.5-acre block of the site. Leathery, earthy and grippy, it has a wealth of tangy red-fruit and floral aromatics, the core tightly structured and youthful, with fresh, high-toned acidity.

name: Cobb 2017 Doc's Ranch Swan and Calera Selection Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
designation: Doc's Ranch Swan and Calera Selection
review: Juicy red fruit invites one into an exuberantly perfumed explosion of grip, earth and black tea. With youthful tannin and focused structure, it unwinds slowly in the glass, unfurling further flavors of exotic spice and mineral.

name: Cobb 2017 Rice-Spivak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Pungent compostable earth and forest pine form a foundation of savory sensation across the palate of this lively vineyard-designate, brightly intertwined in pomegranate and rhubarb. Grippy and spicy in both baking and Asian spice, it impresses across a lengthy finish.

name: Clos du Val 2017 Estate Pinot Noir (Carneros)
company: Clos Du Val Wine/Goelet Wine Estates
review: Char and gamy meat accent a thick, funky and tangy expression of velvety concentration in this estate wine. Baking spice and forest floor stand up to a thicket of cherry cola.

name: Claiborne & Churchill 2017 Twin Creeks Estate Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Black plum and dark berries meet with concentrated wood aromas on this richer bottling from the Edna Valley. The palate comes alive with tangy cranberry, pomegranate-syrup and orange-rind flavors, finishing with a touch of eucalyptus oil.

name: Claiborne & Churchill 2017 Greengate Ranch & Vineyard Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Slightly reductive aromas of black cherry, plum, forest floor and wild sage show on the nose of this bottling. There's a leathery elegance to the palate, where tangy red-plum and cranberry flavors arise.

name: Claiborne & Churchill 2017 Classic Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Light and bright aromas of rose petal and strawberry gain complexity through hints of leather, game and curry leaf on the nose of this bottling. The palate combines cranberry, raspberry and pomegranate with a hint of gamy meat, framed by chalky tannins.

name: Chloe 2017 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Dark-red-fruit and boysenberry aromas meet with wet gravel on the nose of this bottling. The palate is tart with dark-red fruit, and there's a hint of damp earth as well.

name: Chateau St. Jean 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This attractive and well-made wine offers a nice combination of red cherry and light fresh herb flavors that are accented by hints of wood smoke. It's a medium-bodied, palate-cleansing and easy-to-enjoy wine.

name: Chasing Lions 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Leafy aromas lead to rich, almost-sweet and aggressively spicy flavors that stand out like additions. It's essentially a simple, soft wine dressed with spices.
