name: Charles Krug 2017 Pinot Noir (Napa Valley)
review: An intensity of tannin and sweetness of oak power this wine, which is unabashedly fruity in blueberry and dark cherry. Caramel, cola and vanilla ride along on the finish.

name: Chamisal 2017 Califa Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: This is an attention-grabbing wine, clicking most of the Pinot Noir boxes, from dark fruit and exotic spice to earth and game. Aromas of roasted meats mix with Luxardo cherry and star anise on the nose. The rich palate begins with black-cherry foam and cardamom cream before the lavish tannins and underlying acidity round out the finish.

name: Challen 2017 Zotovich Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: There's a tart boysenberry start to the nose of this bottling, which also offers root beer spice and a pinch of sagebrush. Black-plum and baking-spice flavors converge on the palate, where wild herbs also pop on the finish.

name: Challen 2017 Bentrock Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tangy red-plum skins meet with savory peppercorn and dried thyme aromas on the nose of this bottling. The palate is also very tangy with tart red fruits, enhanced by a clove spice.

name: Challen 2017 La Encantada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Fresh hibiscus, crushed cranberry and cinnamon spice show on this bottling, which is quite light in the glass. It's light and brisk on the palate, with tart cranberry and raspberry flavors that are dusted by pepper and thyme.

name: Challen 2017 Dierberg Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Brilliant aromas of red cherry, mossy forest floor and crushed slate are deliciously bright yet also complex on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. There's a pop of fresh strawberry fruit to start the sip, followed by waves of toasted sage, sesame seed and wild thyme, with acid that pokes out on the finish.

name: Challen 2017 Tondre H Block Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Savory lines of soy decorate the dried rose-petal and earthy dried mulberry aromas on the nose of this bottling. The palate is quite complex, combining roasted plums, cranberry sauce, orange rind, nutmeg and warm pastry flavors.

name: Challen 2017 Peake Ranch Calera Clone Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dark roasted cherry and baked plum aromas are spiced up by seasoning salts and smoked meat scents on the nose of this clonal expression. Dark-plum flavors consume the concentrated palate.

name: Chalk Hill 2017 Estate Bottled Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Bright waves of strawberry, cranberry and pomegranate make an immediate impression in this robust, full-bodied wine. Accents of forest floor and baking spice add to the mix of complex flavor and texture.

name: Center of Effort 2017 Prova 612017 Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: There's a prominent amount of oak on this single-block, single-clone bottling, leading to aromas of roasted wood and smoke alongside hibiscus and cranberry. Oak-roasted strawberry flavors meet with a penetrating acidity on the palate.

name: Center of Effort 2017 Effort Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Dark and intense aromas of candied cherry and boysenberry are lifted by pinches of sage, thyme and wood spice on the nose of this bottling. There is a black-raspberry snap to the sip, where more wild chaparral herbs add nuance.

name: Cellars 33 2017 Hummingbird Hill Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a site near Taylor Mountain on the outskirts of the Petaluma Gap appellation, this red is light in color and delicate in a floral aroma. Earthy elements of sage and black tea underlie crisp, elegant flavors of cherry, pomegranate and blood orange. A lasting finish of baking spice caps it off.

name: Cellars 33 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Pungently earthy in compost and black tea, this wine is richly layered, with juicy, complementary flavors of boysenberry and black cherry. From two sites along opposite sides of the appellation, it combines power with stemmy tension and grace.

name: Cebada 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Dark cherry aromas meet with dried oregano, thyme and tangy plum-skin aromas on the nose of this bottling. Roasted plum and dark-berry flavors meet with dried garden herbs on the palate, which is pleasantly tangy and wrapped in a taut structure.

name: Cebada 2017 Cuvée Pinot Noir (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: A heady mix of tangy black plum, rust and garam masala spice shows on the nose of this bottling. There's fresh but dark boysenberry fruit on the palate alongside violet and rose petals, finishing on an ample baking-spice note.

name: Castello di Amorosa 2017 Three Arrows Ranch Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Gamy elements of deli meat and tight, potent tannins mark this wine, which is elegantly restrained in style. Tangy cranberry, strawberry and orange flavors emphasize its overall brightness and lasting notes of dried herb.

name: Castello di Amorosa 2017 Terra De Promissio Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Earth, tea and mushroom flavors meet a thick, woody center of pomegranate and citrus peel in this full-bodied, ripe and rich wine. A touch of bright, buzzy acidity keeps it relatively balanced and fresh.

name: Castello di Amorosa 2017 Morning Dew Ranch Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Tempting fruit flavors run deep and long in this full-bodied, focused and concentrated wine made from a vineyard established by Burt Williams of Williams Selyem. Black cherry and rhubarb ring clear as a bell while subtle clove, ginger and cinnamon add complexity. A mouthfilling texture and lingering finish complete the picture.

name: Carucci 2017 Greengate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Elegant aromas of violet and lilac greet the nose on this bottling and then come deeper, heartier touches of mulberry, exotic plum and fennel pollen. There is a fresh zip to the sip, where alpine strawberry, tangerine rind, dried herb and floral flavors all sing together, though may need another year in bottle to find true harmony.

name: Cartograph 2017 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This vineyard blend from the Cartograph estate and other sites is textured, complex, spicy and herbal. It is light bodied in style, with elegant, silky tannins and shows bright acidity alongside flavors of rhubarb, pomegranate and red cherry.

name: Carmel Road 2017 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Dried cherry, leather and baked earth aromas arise on the nose of this widely available, fairly priced bottling. There are earthy tones to the palate as well, where dried meat flavors meet with soft cherry and strawberry tones.

name: Caraccioli 2017 Escolle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Slightly leathery and rustic aromas of dark cherry, turned earth and dried herbs show on the nose of this bottling. A compelling texture carries herbal flavors of dried thyme and marjoram, which decorate the tangy plum-fruit core.

name: Cambria 2017 Barbara's Clone 667 Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Black-plum, rose-petal and violet aromas converge on the nose of this clonal selection from a historic vineyard. There's an earthy array to the sip, where leathery and loamy flavors meet with tart cranberry tones.

name: Cambria 2017 Julia's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: This excellent Pinot offers aromas of dried cherry, toasted sage and roasted strawberry that lead into a palate that's snappy and light, yet with tons of weighty stuffing. Flavors of bright raspberry and wild thyme lead into a vanilla-kissed finish.

name: Cambria 2017 Julia's Vineyard Signature Collection Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Tangy red-cherry and plum-skin aromas are leveled by slight hickory smoke and a pinch of wild herbs on the nose of this top-tier bottling from a large estate winery. The plummy flavors of the palate are cut by green peppercorns, which provide an almost extreme level of spice.

name: Calera 2017 de Villiers Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mt. Harlan)
review: Roasted boysenberry, crushed stone, baked earth and a slight plum-skin tang show on the nose of this bottling from vines planted in 1996. There's a warm-weather sense on the palate, where cooked berry flavors dominate, yet the acid and rocky tension keep it complex and intense.

name: Calera 2017 Jensen Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mt. Harlan)
review: Shy aromas of black plum, mace and rusty earth show on the nose of this bottling. Dried cherry and cranberry flavors meet with dried mushroom on the palate, which is elegant while mellow.

name: Calera 2017 Muns Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: It's rare for this historic winery to produce a single-vineyard expression from beyond the estate, but this bottling is a good argument for doing more. The rugged nose starts smoked cranberry, orange-rind and oak aromas, leading into a tense palate of sandalwood, strawberry, rusty earth and baking spice.

name: Calera 2017 Reed Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mt. Harlan)
review: There's a stunning purity of intense Bing cherry fruit that hits the nose first on this bottling, and then the region's expected waves of wild sage, dank forest herbs and spicy peppercorn converge as well. Black sage oil makes for a sappy palate, which surrounds a cranberry and pomegranate core.

name: Calera 2017 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: This blend from sites across the Central Coast carries all the expert hallmarks of the brand, from ripe fruit to earthy spice. Tightly wound aromas of pomegranate, berry, soil and star anise lead into a crisp yet savory palate of raspberry and deep cherry. Pinches of dried sage and thyme linger on the finish.

name: Cabana Wines 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This smooth wine coats ripe plum and cherry flavors with a butter and vanilla overlay. Soft and almost-sweet in mouthfeel, the wine feels full bodied and low in acidity.

name: Bydand 2017 Umino Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Dark and rich in blackberry and cherry, this wine is lightly dusted in cardamom and cinnamon, with bold intensity and lasting richness. The site shows through in its elegance and power.

name: Bydand 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Earthy in black tea, forest floor and clove, this appellation wine has a sublime texture, balanced weight and lasting heat.

name: Bucher 2017 Opa's Block Bucher Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Only a tiny amount of this block-designate was made, the wine showing delicate, nuanced notes of earthy black tea and tobacco. The savory components mesh well against the fruity blueberry and dark cherry, finishing silky smooth and spiced in nutmeg.

name: Bucher 2017 Bucher Vineyard Pommard Clone Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Rich and round in juicy black cherry and strawberry, this full-bodied wine is concentrated and intense, with complex layers of baking spice and clove. The oak is supportive, accentuating elements of vanilla and cola.

name: Bruliam 2017 Sangiacomo Roberts Road Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: Earthy black tea coats a core of supple tannin and fresh acidity in this well-made, light-bodied wine from the cool site. Forest-floor notes ride alongside dark brooding black-cherry and strawberry fruit that presents delicately and with length.

name: Bruliam 2017 Soberanes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Baked black raspberry, rose petals tea leaves, smoked sage and a hint of blood show on the complex nose of this bottling. It lands with weight and chalky texture on the palate, which is full of roasted plum and sagebrush flavors, leading into a finish wrapped in sizzling acidity and vanilla-cookie spice.

name: Bruliam 2017 Gap's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: Earthy in tea, rhubarb and dark cherry, this is a rich and full-bodied explosion of flavor that sizzles in acidity and racy energy. The oak and tannin are well behaved and integrated, with woody after effects of forest floor.

name: Bridlewood 2017 Reserve Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Rich black cherry, macerated plums and crème brûlée aromas invite the nose into this richer expression of Pinot Noir. The palate takes that dark fruit and throws in cappuccino foam and toasty oak, bringing a pleasant hint of power to the sip.

name: Brick Barn 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Quite light in the glass, this bottling offers mild raspberry and mint aromas on the nose. There's a sizzle of acidity on the sip as well as crunchy cranberry and potpourri flavors.

name: Brassfield 2017 Pinot Noir (High Valley)
review: Very smooth and supple, this full-bodied wine combines warm baking-spice aromas with black-cherry and mulled plum flavors. It is full bodied and firmed up slightly by light tannins.

name: Bouchaine 2017 Dijon Clone Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Silky and earthy, this wine starts off as relatively reserved, taking some time to open. Generous tannins present around layers of dark cherry and black tea.

name: Boheme 2017 Occidental Hills Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Dense tannins gird a robust core of toasted oak and black tea in this coastal wine. Sturdy elements of forest floor and clove complement wild strawberry and raspberry flavors and reach a cohesive whole.

name: Boheme 2017 English Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Rose-petal-potpourri aromas lead to a palate of baked plum, dried currant and white pepper in this vineyard-designate from the coast. Racy acidity boosts the balanced richness and concentration, finishing in toasted oak and black tea.

name: Boheme 2017 Taylor Ridge Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Herbal and evocative of forest floor and bay, this is a juicy and well-structured wine, made in small amounts from a coastal site. Rhubarb, pomegranate and strawberry flavors mingle around a core of bright acidity and grippy texture, with a saline edge.

name: Bogle 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Seductive and spicy, this full-bodied, abundantly flavorful wine combines ripe black fruits with attractive wood smoke, clove and anise nuances. It's mouthfilling and rich, but well balanced by acidity and light tannins.

name: Böen 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: A rich boysenberry-pie aroma is complemented by licorice, caramel, cinnamon and cream on the nose of this ripe Pinot Noir by Joseph Wagner. Candied blackberry, clove and star anise show on the thick palate, which is more about power than finesse.

name: Böen 2017 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Quite dark in the glass, this bottling is extremely rich and heavy, starting with aromas of black cherry, caramel, gingerbread and waffle cone. It's thick and jammy on the palate, with tangy black cherry and powerful licorice spice.

name: Boekenoogen 2017 3 Clone Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Ripe black cherries, vanilla, clove, crushed slate and oak show on the nose of this bottling. There is flashy acid on the sip, where black-plum-skin, snappy pomegranate and white-pepper flavors make for a refreshing yet layered experience.

name: Blue Farm 2017 Anne Katherina Farmhouse Estate Farmed Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Beautifully crafted and layered in delicate red fruit, this estate wine is light in nutmeg, sage and dried herb, a savory landscape against which silky tannins adhere. Rose-petal aromas last throughout, underscoring its beguiling nature.

name: Blue Farm 2017 1861 Vineyard Estate Farmed Pinot Noir (Sonoma Valley)
review: With plenty of influence from the San Pablo Bay at 1,000-feet-elevation, this site contributes high-toned acidity to the wine as well as a persistence of savory forest floor, allspice and white pepper. With substantial weight and concentration, it unwinds slowly in the glass, offering crunchy red fruit along the way.

name: Blue Farm 2017 King Ridge Vineyard Estate Farmed Pinot Noir (Fort Ross-Seaview)
review: From high-elevation slopes overlooking the ocean, this powerhouse wine is intensely built and structured for aging. Youthful tannins offer fistfuls of grip, providing a foundation for dense tones of black cherry, earth and black tea. Enjoy from 2027–2030.

name: Blue Farm 2017 Anne Katherina Vineyard Estate Farmed Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Tangy berry fruit is laced in black tea, earth and lifted acidity in this stellar wine, the personal project of Anne Moller-Racke, who long ran Donum Estate. It is intensely full in feel and youthfully tannic, offering a wall of defined structure. This will do well over time, so enjoy from 2025–2030.

name: Bliss 2017 Estate Bottled Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: This wine is not openly fruity, but firm-textured and appetizing, offering moderate tannins and good acidity that frame modest black-cherry and plum flavors. These begin to open with a little time in the glass.

name: Black Kite 2017 Soberanes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Dark aromas of roasted plum, loamy earth, crushed rocks and a hint of shiitake mushroom show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is loaded with earthy flavors of rock and mushroom, set against a backdrop of boysenberry and wild herbs.

name: Black Kite 2017 Sierra Mar Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of rust, dried cherry and potpourri meet with a light vanilla spice on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Light cranberry and pomegranate flavors make for a rather delicate and tightly woven sip.

name: Black Kite 2017 River Turn Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Hints of roasted beets and rhubarb join the seductive black-cherry aromas in this full-bodied, concentrated and distinctive wine. It shows very good balance and moderate tannins, so there's no great hurry to drink it. Best through 2026.

name: Black Kite 2017 Stony Terrace Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Concentration and density are hallmarks of this full-bodied, firmly tannic wine from a consistent estate-oriented producer. It is well packed, with black-cherry, cedar and violet flavors. Aged in two-thirds new French barrels, its slightly tight mouthfeel will help it age; best through 2026.

name: Black Kite 2017 Redwoods' Edge Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This full-bodied and firmly structured wine is nicely packed with black-fruit flavors and accented with enticing cinnamon, nutmeg and cedar notes that emerge on the palate and last on the finish.

name: Black Kite 2017 Kite's Rest Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Beautifully balanced and elegant, this medium- to full-bodied wine serves up flavors of black cherry, blackberry and black tea backed by tingly acidity and moderate fine-grained tannins. It's just short of fully ripe, bringing an appetizing sense of restraint to the palate and finish.

name: Black Kite 2017 Gap's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is a beautifully layered and complex wine from the well-known site, all from one Dijon clone of the grape. Weighty and expansive on the palate, it opens in rose petal and compost, evolving to show velvety texture and tannin and bright, crisp pomegranate and dark cherry.

name: Birichino 2017 Boer Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chalone)
review: Smoke, toasted, crisp red-plum, wild-sagebrush and gamy meat aromas converge on the layered nose of this bottling. The palate is equally complex, as toasted sage battles for attention against wild-plum, cranberry and roasted red-apple flavors.

name: Big Basin 2017 Coastview Vineyard Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Tart strawberry, plum extract, crushed stone and pinches of herb and soil show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Dried herbs, dried hibiscus, tea leaves and light red-currant flavors ride bright acidity into the finish.

name: Big Basin 2017 Alfaro Family Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Trampled herbs, forest-floor, juniper-berry and cranberry aromas reflect the forest-ringed setting of many vineyards in the Corralitos area. Curry leaf, bay and juniper flavors make for a vividly spicy palate, where hints of dried cranberry and cherry linger in the background.

name: Big Basin 2017 Old Corral Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: A slightly muddy brick color in the glass, this earthy bottling begins with bay and tea-leaf aromas alongside rust, juniper and cranberry. The wild character paints the palate as well, where pine-needle, dried red-currant and orange-rind flavors make for a vivid sip.

name: Big Basin 2017 Lester Family Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Quite light and slightly clouded in the glass, this bottling begins with tart aromas of raspberry and cranberry, bolstered by amazing amounts of green sage, wild peppercorn and crushed pine needle. The wildness consumes the palate, where bay leaf decorates the wild, woody berry flavors.

name: Betwixt 2017 Lester Family Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Very light in the glass, this wine begins with tart pomegranate and pine-needle aromas on the nose. There's a great snap to the palate, where tangy raspberry flavors are spiced by white pepper and dried rose petals.

name: Benovia 2017 Cohn Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: From an estate site planted in 1970, this juicy, concentrated wine has savory streaks of black tea and rhubarb. The layers of texture and tannic grip frame flavors of forest floor and dried herb that linger on the finish.

name: Belle Glos 2017 Eulenloch Pinot Noir (Napa Valley)
review: Mint and sage lead the way to a viscous, velvety and somewhat syrupy concentration of overtly ripe, fleshy black cherry and cola in this bold, brawny wine. The oak is pronounced.

name: Belle Glos 2017 Dairyman Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Made in a thick and robust style, this hearty red is powerful and weighty in oak and tannin. Spicy seasonings of cinnamon and clove highlight rich, viscous flavors of dark cherry and berry, with rose aromatics up front and soothing.

name: Belle Glos 2017 Las Alturas Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Very dark in the glass, this is rich, heady wine that offers powerfully hedonistic flavors yet does not retain much Pinot Noir-ness. Strong aromas of oak, black cherry, cola and caramel lead into a thick palate of berry paste, root beer and crème brûlée.

name: Bellante 2017 Dierberg Vineyard Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Quite light in the glass, this bottling is very earthy and rustic in style, offering aromas of roasted cranberry, toasted sage, potting soil and a hint of iron. The roasted red-fruit and chaparral-spice flavors carry into the medium-bodied palate, which is loaded with acid.

name: Bellante 2017 Bentrock Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dark-berry and black-plum skin aromas are lifted by clove and nutmeg aromas on the nose of this bottling from a vineyard on the appellation's southwest corner. A firm tannic structure upholds flavors of roasted plum and brown spice on the dried fruit palate.

name: Bellante 2017 Mon Sheri Reserve Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Black-cherry, roasted berry, loamy soil and soy aromas show on he nose of this richer spin on Pinot Noir. The wine lands with a dry structure, carrying deep flavors of black raspberry, mulberry and a sage-like spice.

name: Bellante 2017 Vieux Chêne Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Dark fruits of blackberry and boysenberry meet with a bit of licorice, potting soil and light sage on the nose of this bottling, whose name translates to “old oak.” It pops a bit more on the sip, with fresh black-raspberry and purple-flower flavors.

name: Bella Impavida 2017 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Rather light-bodied and enticingly fruity, this wine sprinkles baking spices over ripe cherry and plum flavors while the soft, melting texture feels almost sweet.

name: Belden Barns 2017 Serendipity Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)
review: From a field blend planted to nine clones, this whole-cluster red is savory and stemmy, with an endearing earthiness. Black fruit dominates, a mix of currant and cherry as soft integrated oak emphasizes a thread of nutmeg and cardamom.

name: Beauregard 2017 Coast Grade Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Ryan Beauregard is making some of the better wines in California right now, and this herbally rich bottling is solid evidence. Aromas of sage, tarragon and light menthol mix with brisk and earthy cherry and savory smoke on the nose. The palate packs the minty herbs with lush red fruit, providing a wine that's both rich and green-tinged at once.

name: Beauregard 2017 Beauregard Ranch Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Dried cherry, minty redwood, wild sage and porcini mushroom aromas make for an earthy entry to the nose of this bottling. The palate continues in line, combining rusty sanguine flavors with dried cranberry, dried tarragon and crushed rock flavors that lead into a snickerdoodle-kissed finish.

name: Barden 2017 Sanford & Benedict Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tense aromas of elderberry, crushed black rocks and singed tobacco make for a very unique nose on this bottling. There is plenty of acidic zest to the palate, where fresh black-raspberry, light pepper-spice and very earthy soil and smashed rock flavors converge.

name: Banshee Wines 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: Sharp, tangy red fruit is complemented by earthy dried herb, forest and clove spice—the wine intensely built and opulent on the palate. It offers good value for the price, a well-made offering of varietal approachability.

name: Banshee 2017 Marine Layer Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Tones of earthy stems and forest floor are complemented by brighter bursts of strawberry, orange peel and rose petal in this medium-bodied wine. Velvety on the palate, it's smooth yet offers lasting grip.

name: Bannister 2017 Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Silky smooth and robust in tannin, this wine offers nuanced red fruit, baking spice and black tea—characteristics that make it appealingly varietal. A lift of acidity brightens the finish.

name: B Cellars 2017 Manzana Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: With aromatics of rose and cola, this wine is sharp and tangy, with high-toned red fruit and lasting opulence. Its velvety richness underscores a full-bodied approach and structure, with a fleshy finish of baking spice.

name: B Cellars 2017 Calesa Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Juicy and exotic, this wine is bright, with a depth of underlying richness that matches well. Black cherry, brambly blackberry, black tea and peppercorn give it an intriguing appeal of savory fruit finished off in fresh acidity.

name: B Cellars 2017 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Perfumed, floral and intensely oaky and tannic on entry, this full-bodied wine develops to show nuanced, underlying freshness and intriguing elements of forest floor and dried herb. Red- and black-cherry compote add richness to the whole, finding a length of cola spice on the long finish.

name: Artesa 2017 Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Los Carneros)
review: Delicate, crunchy red fruit is lifted by a floral accent in this estate-farmed wine. Crushed rock and iron components add complexity and texture to a rounded, rich and balanced expression of the variety.

name: Artesa 2017 Pinot Noir (Los Carneros)
review: This is full in body, fleshy and widely appealing in style. It's delicately textured in silky tannins, with flavors of cranberry, orange and toasted vanilla. It finishes on tones of cinnamon and cola.

name: Armida 2017 Chalone Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chalone)
review: Roasted black-cherry, cola spice, maple-syrup and brown-sugar aromas show on the nose of this very accessible bottling from an appellation whose wines are usually much tighter and reserved at this stage. Star anise, baked strawberry and even melon flavors arise on the sip.

name: Argus 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: With a rich entry of dark cherry and plum, this approachable red is soft, fleshy and round, with wide appeal. Integrated tannin and oak provide a cushy opulence that underscores its jammy exuberance.

name: Apriori 2017 Wentzel Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This deep-colored and full-bodied wine offers a distinctive blend of black-fruit flavors accompanied by forest floor and slightly smoky undertones that add nice complexity. With time in the glass dried plum and black-tea nuances come out and the mouthfeel melts into a very smooth texture.

name: Angeline 2017 Reserve Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Tasty and well balanced, this wine will hit the spot for all kinds of occasions. It is lightly smoky in aroma, full of red and black cherry on the palate, medium bodied and has a tangy mouthfeel.

name: Ancien 2017 Fiddlestix Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Aromas of black raspberry, ripe plum, licorice and spicy wild herbs show on the nose of this delicious bottling. It's rich and lush on the palate, where dark baked-fruit flavors are enhanced by star anise, cardamom and chai spices, all wrapped in fabulously creamy tannins.

name: Ancien 2017 Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Lively in brooding black fruit, this wine is balanced in richness and length, with a lasting wealth of baking spice and black tea. Well integrated, it forms a cohesive experience on the smooth, seductive palate, finding a balance across all its components.

name: Ancien 2017 Jouissance Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Bright, fresh and tannic, with earthy layers of black tea, sharp red fruit and a tangy length of spice, this displays the appellation well, combining full-bodied richness and weight with underlying acidity that balances the breadth.

name: Ancien 2017 Mink Vineyard Pinot Noir (Coombsville)
review: This is a perennially stunning wine and this vintage is no exception. Spicy earth and black cherry intertwine on the nose and palate as additional waves of strawberry and rhubarb weigh in. Medium bodied, balanced and complex, the wine's texture is creamy and has plenty of length, with stony minerality throughout.

name: Ancien 2017 Toyon Farm Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Very little of this wine was made. It is perfumed and woody, with a thick midpalate of pomegranate, cherry and citrus, flavors that meld around well-integrated acidity and oak.

name: Anaba 2017 Las Brisas Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Light, bright and velvety on the palate, with delicate acidity, this is a well-made, memorable red from the great site, dazzling in cranberry and orange.
